Company Description:
Acclaro is derived from the Latin verb "acclaro," which means to reveal or, more specifically, to clarify, reveal, amplify and decide — an ideal name for our consulting firm.

Acclaro’s mission is to help our middle market clients in the M&A ecosystem focus on the right companies (with eyes wide open to the opportunities and the risks); and help management teams focus on the best options for growth, post-close. 

We ensure our loyal cadre of clients has the highest ROIs in the industry, by consistently generating actionable insights to support their investment decisions. We are focused on outcomes, by linking research and analysis to solutions. 

Our role is to serve as more than just data gatherers — we deliver options, ideas, and actionable insights to directly address your decision points.

We work together, as if we are partners down the hall. We communicate frequently. If there is bad news, it travels fast. We are an extension of your team.

Company Description:
Adasina Social Capital (“Adasina”) is an investment and financial activism firm that serves as a critical bridge between financial markets and social justice movements for the benefit of people and our planet. Adasina uses four interlocking levers to enact large-scale, systemic change: People, Investments, Campaigns, and Education.

We believe that transformed systems are best created by people outside of traditional power structures who carry new perspectives. By building a diverse team that reflects the communities for which we seek justice, we created a new kind of investing based on the needs of those most impacted by our existing inequitable systems. In partnership with social justice organizations within these communities, we define the criteria that guide our investments. We use this community-sourced wisdom to educate and mobilize other investors with campaigns that amplify the needs, perspectives, and voices of impacted communities throughout the financial system.

We believe that community-sourced impact data should set the standards for how companies and governments participate in racial, gender, economic, and climate justice. With Adasina, investors can seek both financial and social returns that are accountable to the well-being of the people and planet they impact. 

Adasina Social Capital is the sister company of Robasciotti & Philipson, a registered investment advisory firm with over 15 years of experience creating social justice portfolios for clients. We are majority-owned and operated by women, people of color, and members of the LGBTQ+ community. Both companies are headquartered in San Francisco and maintain client relationships throughout the United States.

Important Disclosure: https://buff.ly/2FRCVy2

We also comply with SEC regulations and therefore do not accept recommendations or endorsements on any of our social media channels.

Title: Operating Partner & Fractional Executive
Company Product and Services: operating core build, leadership alignment, value creation, operating partner advisory, ebitda improvement, m&a integration, turnaround management, ceo advisory, mid-market operations, pe portfolio operations, family office, performance improvement, exit preparation, pricing strategy, capital allocation, board advisory, fractional cxo, interim executive, interim leadership
Company Description:
Northline Operating Partners works with business owners, family companies, and PE investors who know their business should be performing better. We walk in, identify the root constraints — usually within the first two weeks — and stay until execution moves the numbers. Revenue, margin, cash flow.

Founded by experienced operators, not consultants.

WHERE COMPANIES GET STUCK

Most companies aren't underperforming because of strategy. They're underperforming because the business isn't aligned around what matters.
• Too many priorities competing for time and capital
• Leadership teams busy but not aligned
• Accountability that isn't enforced
• No consistent cadence driving execution

WHAT WE DO

Three core services:
• Operating Core Build — installing the cadence, accountability, and process that turn strategy into execution
• Leadership Alignment — diagnosing misalignment fast and getting the right people in the right seats
• Value Creation — identifying where margin and growth actually live, then building the plan to capture it

HOW WE WORK

Engagements start with a 4-6 week diagnostic: leadership interviews, strategy translation, KPI review, and a direct read on what's working and what isn't. Most clients find the insight alone is worth the investment. From there, engagements move to alignment and execution build (8-12 weeks) and an optional operating partner retainer (6-12 months).

WHO WE WORK WITH

• CEOs and leadership teams whose businesses aren't performing to potential
• PE investors & family-owned businesses driving value creation or preparing for exit
• Companies in leadership transition or between executives

ON AI

We don't lead with AI. Once the operating foundation is solid, we use AI and data to sharpen how the business runs, not to paper over the gaps.

The end result is a business that performs better, is worth more, and can prove it. If we don't move the number, we haven't earned the fee.

Contact: jamie@northlineop.com

To that end, Andra Partners utilizes a rigorous targeted search process leveraging best in class research protocols and resources to identify proprietary acquisition and minority investment opportunities with industry leading middle market companies. These targeted searches support client-defined platform and add-on initiatives, and engage the ownership of middle market companies in confidential dialogues with industry focused acquirers and investors.

As a result, Andra Partners has delivered over $2B in change of control and minority investment opportunities to its clients, and enabled the ownership and leadership of many middle market companies to explore enhanced business growth and wealth capture strategies that further capitalize on their success.

Title: Principal
Company Description: Arroyo Energy Investment Partners LLC is an independent private equity fund manager with an investment focus in infrastructure projects throughout the Americas. Arroyo is based in Houston, Texas with an office in Santiago, Chile, and focuses on power and related infrastructure investments such as natural gas pipelines, conventional and renewable power generation and energy storage assets. Deep industry experience, strong customer relationships and in-house asset management are just some of what Arroyo believes sets it apart from its peers. The firm currently manages investments in the United States, Mexico and Chile.

Title: Partner & Chief Operating Officer
Company Description:
Informing Change is an Oakland, California-based strategic learning and consulting team. For nearly 30 years, our team has been built on a foundation of diverse perspectives, experiences, and backgrounds. That diversity is our strength. What unites all of us is a core belief that when those most impacted by a challenge come together to inform strategy, they build the power to bring transformative change to their communities.

The pursuit of our company values—curiosity, integrity, compassion, and social justice — guides everything we do. We embrace the wide range of communities, clients, foci, and contexts in which we work, bringing our core principles and practices to each project to inform and affirm the power of those we work with. Our company values are put into practice by centering equity and supporting those working to end racism and intersectional oppressions, discriminatory practices and policies, and inequitable systems. 

At Informing Change, we believe that change is:

•	Informed By Learning: We believe learning is essential to the growth and development needed to achieve well-being for all. Learning is about insight, which comes from rearranging and reconsidering what we know in light of new information and perspectives. Vitally, it is also about developing strategies to apply that insight.

•	Rooted in Power: Through this lens, we understand that power dynamics, inequity, and histories of discrimination may impact what counts as credible evidence or who counts as informed enough to have a voice in decision-making. We acknowledge and actively challenge historically prevalent biases and approach our projects using equitable evaluation  and culturally responsive  frameworks.

•	Attentive to Systems: We operate with the conviction that meaningful and lasting change requires a focus on the systems and contexts within which people navigate their lives. We believe all communities and individuals should have a voice in defining their own outcomes.

Title: Partner
Company Product and Services: private equity, partnership, culture, minority investments, control investments, growth equity, capital raises, recapitalizations, consumer/retail, software, business services, industrial, healthcare
Company Description:
GreyLion (formerly known as PWP Growth Equity) focuses on investing in high-growth businesses in the lower middle market. GreyLion’s investment strategy is based on the collective 80 years of experience of the senior team. Our goal is to find quality investment opportunities where we partner with the business owners and management teams of lower middle market companies across the consumer, industrial, healthcare, software and services sectors, primarily in the United States. We target equity investments between $25 million and $125 million. We will consider both minority and control investments in a variety of structures, including growth capital raises, recapitalizations and management-led buyouts.

We seek to invest in businesses that have strong and differentiated competitive positions in their respective industries that with the leadership of strong management teams, offer the potential for significant continued growth.

Title: Founder & CEO
Company Product and Services: m&a advisory, strategic advisory, founder transitions, succession planning, lower middle market, service businesses, density driven businesses, recapitalization, capital structure
Company Description:
Mutatio Advisory is a boutique M&A and strategic advisory firm built for founders and families who own service businesses in the lower middle market.

We focus on density-driven service businesses — companies with shared value drivers and consolidation economics, such as waste and recycling, HVAC, landscape, pest control, fire and life safety, security services, facilities services, uniform and linen, government contracting, collision repair, quick lube and tire service, ADAS calibration, and density-driven health care services.

Most owners we work with are weighing one of four paths: grow, recapitalize, transition, or exit. The work is to think clearly about which path is right, then run the process honestly. No predetermined outcome, no pressure toward a deal that doesn't serve the owner, no learning on someone else's company.

Mutatio is founder-led by Bob Fowler, who brings 40 years in principal investing and leveraged finance, and has led or overseen transactions totaling more than $6 billion in enterprise value. The firm is based in Boston at 53 State Street.

Independent advice without a predetermined destination.


Mutatio. From the Latin — transformation. That's the work.

Title: Principal
Company Product and Services: private equity, credit opportunities, equity strategy
Company Description:
Eos is an alternative investment firm with approximately $1.5 billion of capital under management.  Eos is an active investor in the credit, public equity and private equity markets and has a long/short credit strategy dedicated to socially responsible investing. 

PRIVATE EQUITY
Our goal is to partner with superior management teams and commit our professionals' collective business experience, network of relationships, strategic judgment, and financial markets expertise in order to elevate these businesses to the next level.

CREDIT OPPORTUNITIES
Eos Credit Opportunities is a value-oriented, long-biased credit fund with a proven track record of investing within the liquid credit spectrum through multiple credit cycles.

EQUITY STRATEGIES
Eos Focused Equity employs a long-biased, value oriented equity strategy with a core focus on event-driven and special situations, turnarounds, post bankruptcies, and recapitalizations.

Title: Principal
Company Product and Services: expense reduction, group purchasing, profit enhancement, cost containment, spend management, expense reduction analysis, supplier negotiations, profit recovery, purchasing program, reverse auctions, private equity cost reduction, private equity consulting, pe group savings, private equity expenses, corporate expense reduction, pe portfolio services, pe consulting
Company Description:
Alliance Cost Containment (ACC) assists corporations, private equity backed companies and industry groups with profit improvement by lowering non-core procurement costs.We provide cost reduction services that allows organizations to maximize operating revenue and save up to 35% across 35+ common expense categories. ACC aggregates purchasing volume to offer buying power on scale that most companies cannot achieve on their own. As a result, clients across different industries see impressive, sustainable savings. 

Our model is performance based with fees based on delivered, audited results. This means we take our ethics and transparency very seriously. Our ability to document results is what sets us apart. Clients are guided through the process with minimal time needed from their internal resources. We employ a highly specialized team that reviews your current purchasing activity and negotiates with providers to get the lowest market rates.

Alliance Cost Containment has perfected the process of reducing indirect operating expenses. We work efficiently to expertly analyze data and manage a bidding process that reduces operating costs in a matter of weeks. Our market knowledge, national purchasing agreements, unique subject matter experts and monthly audits set us apart. We look beyond the numbers at the suppliers themselves. At ACC it is not just about the lowest price – it’s about achieving — and tracking — top quality, service and value.

Clearbrook develops customized investment solutions for institutions, family offices and high-net-worth individuals. Independent and privately held, we serve as advisor and advocate for our clients, with capabilities ranging from full discretionary management (Outsourced CIO) ongoing advisory services, and specialized programs.

Clearbrook's business model is built on fresh, independent thinking, disciplined research, and vision tempered with experience.  Our culture is client driven, focusing on customized solutions for each client's unique objectives and needs.  We bring actionable insights to every client interaction.  We don't just come in with reports and credentials - we come in with ideas. Company Description:
Our Vision is to empower businesses to build resilience and growth.

“SpecNav’s strategy development and training eco-system enables business leaders to become laser focused on the outcomes they need to drive excellence, resilience and growth. Built upon military and maritime fundamental practices, we enable our business partners to learn, train and excel, becoming industry recognized Subject Matter Experts through SpecNav’s business tools, processes and training.”

Supporting businesses to grow and thrive, even in the face of challenge.
Business resilience is a company’s ability to quickly adapt to disruptions while maintaining continuous operations and safeguarding people, assets, and overall brand equity. In simpler terms, it’s how well a business can bounce back or keep running when things go wrong—whether it’s a natural disaster, cyberattack, supply chain issue, or economic crisis.

We help Small to Medium businesses in Maryland grasp opportunities to grow and thrive, enhancing their operational excellence and business capabilities.

Title: Director, Portfolio Allocation
Company Description:
LAB Quantitative Strategies ("LAB QS”) is a data-driven investment firm innovating at the intersection of alpha and beta. We are built on a foundational belief: that well-designed, systematic portfolios can help investors avoid reactive, emotionally driven decisions, especially during periods of market volatility. The name LAB, short for Liquid Alpha + Beta, reflects our commitment to combining sophisticated alpha strategies with liquid, risk-managed beta exposure. Founded in 2019 as a spinout from one of the world’s largest and most respected family offices, LAB QS was established to deliver institutional-quality investing to a broader set of sophisticated investors. LAB QS aims to deliver strong returns with lower volatility and enhanced downside protection through our absolute return and relative return strategies. Today, LAB QS manages over $1.7 billion, trades in 150+ global markets, and partners with leading investment talent around the world.

Title: Founder & CEO
Company Description:
Big 4 Thinking. Founder-First

StackedCFO is a fractional CFO advisory firm built for ambitious entrepreneurs and founders who are serious about scaling - and need financial infrastructure that can keep up.

Founded by Casper, a CPA with 13+ years across PwC, BDO, and CFGI, StackedCFO brings Big 4-caliber financial leadership to growth-stage companies without the full-time executive price tag.

We work with founders doing $200K–$5M in revenue who are tired of making critical business decisions without reliable financial data - and who see $10M, $50M, or more ahead.

What we do:
We design and run your financial infrastructure - cash flow forecasting, KPI reporting, close cycle optimization, M&A readiness, and AI-powered automation - so you can focus on building the business, not decoding spreadsheets.

Why StackedCFO:
Most fractional CFOs come from industry. Our founder came up through Big 4 audit and PE advisory — sitting across from CFOs, Boards, and private equity sponsors on some of the most complex transactions in the market. That rigor is now available to founders who are building real, ambitious companies.
We're based in Boston, MA and work with founders nationwide.

📅 Book a free 30-minute discovery call: stackedcfo.com

Company Description:
Chatsworth Securities LLC is a leading investment banking firm dedicated to empowering the vision of institutional clients worldwide. With a comprehensive suite of services centered around advisory and private capital raising, we have built a strong track record in both domestic and international M&A transactions, raising over $6 billion for traditional and alternative asset managers, and serving as underwriters in hundreds of equity public offerings.

We have realized up to 600 IPOs, follow-ons, and secondary offerings across a wide range of industries, with particular expertise in high-growth sectors such as Technology, Artificial Intelligence, Renewable and Clean Energy, ESG, and Blockchain Technology. Our professionals are also deeply engaged in Private Markets and Alternative Investments, helping clients navigate complex landscapes to seize opportunities and achieve their strategic goals.

At Chatsworth Securities, we take an integrated approach, combining people, ideas, and capital to empower your vision and drive exceptional results. Our team brings substantial experience in assisting domestic and international corporations, asset managers, and entrepreneurial companies in high-impact industries, providing expert guidance on navigating the complexities of today's investment climate.

Founded in 1996, with roots dating back to 1992, Chatsworth Securities is based in Greenwich, CT. Leveraging strong affiliations with top-tier firms, we enhance our research, placement, and banking capabilities, ensuring we remain at the forefront of the investment banking industry — all while empowering our clients to achieve their most ambitious goals in emerging and traditional markets alike.

Title: Managing Partner & Founder
Company Product and Services: capital allocation, roic analysis, working capital optimization, performance improvement, operational due diligence, industrial mid-market, value creation
Company Description:
Sintelo es una firma de Capital Allocation Advisory enfocada en empresas industriales medianas en México.

Ayudamos a dueños de empresas y directores financieros a entender y mejorar su ROIC — el retorno sobre capital invertido — a través de diagnósticos analíticos, implementación operativa y monitoreo continuo.

El número más importante de su empresa no es el EBITDA. Es el ROIC. Y se mueve en dos palancas: margen operativo y rotación de capital. Nuestro trabajo es hacer eso visible — y ejecutable.

Lo que hacemos:
— Diagnóstico de ROIC: calculamos su retorno actual, identificamos las 3 a 5 palancas de mayor impacto y las cuantificamos en pesos.
— Implementación acelerada: valor capturado en 30 a 120 días con cadencia semanal.
— Monitoreo mensual: dashboard de ROIC en tiempo real e informe mensual con el equipo directivo.
— Due diligence operacional: ROIC histórico real y potencial de mejora post-adquisición.

Fundada por Jorge Castro — ex London Consulting Group, Microsoft Azure, Accenture y fundador de Elanboard.

sintelo.com

Title: Operating Partner
Company Description:
Spotlight Equity Partners is a private equity firm focused on acquiring controlling interests in software and technology-enabled services companies headquartered in the United States.

Spotlight partners with and complements portfolio company management teams by providing strategic leadership guided by pragmatic, tactical operating experience. We assume an active role in our portfolio companies, and we draw upon best practices we’ve developed through years of investing and operating experience to improve operations and accelerate value creation at each of our portfolio companies.
 
Spotlight’s operationally-active investment philosophy is reflected in our team, which includes professional investors and seasoned operators. Our team is experienced at executing acquisition transactions, and it possesses substantial operating experience both at an executive management and board level. We work closely with our portfolio company management teams to optimize key functional areas across the entire business. Consequently, we pursue new investment opportunities in which we can contribute both financial capital and operational expertise to help successful companies achieve their full potential.
 
Spotlight invests on behalf of limited partners that include family offices, high net worth individuals, and institutional investors located in the United States and abroad. This long-term capital base affords us the flexibility to focus on value creation over the long run. Through our limited partners, Spotlight has in excess of $250 million of investible capital to deploy into new investments. Since 2007, we have invested over $350 million of equity capital in majority control transactions with eight companies having aggregate enterprise value of more than $1 billion.

Company Description:
Guiding entrepreneurs through their most significant business milestones. Together, we help secure legacy by making the journey personal, impactful, and purpose-driven.

NBB was founded as National Business Brokers in 1978 by Kent Schmidt for the purpose of providing professional M&A advisory, business valuations, and intermediary services. 

In 1984, NBB formed its automotive division. A decade later, Kent's son, Brady, joined the team, creating a legacy of excellence that helped NBB become one of the largest and most experienced dealership M&A advisory firms in the United States. NBB is proud of its leadership role in the automobile industry.

NBB has offices in California, Florida, Michigan, New York, Texas, and Arizona. This regional approach allows us to have national reach while maintaining local market familiarity and representation.  

We are committed to providing best-in-class dealership advisory services that leverage our proven industry expertise and M&A execution capabilities.

Title: Director
Company Product and Services: mergers and acquisitions, sell-side and buy-side, private capital sourcing, strategic advisory, exit planning, investment banking, direct investing, consulting, capital raises, debt raises, pre transaction advisory, transaction preparation
Company Description:
Pinecrest Capital Partners is a premier investment banking firm based in Dallas, Texas that offers exclusive advisory services to middle market companies. Pinecrest’s team of highly experienced professionals operates across a variety of industries and is an expert in managing transaction processes. By focusing on high-touch advisory and one-stop solutions, Pinecrest works to position clients with the goal of maximizing transaction outcomes and realizing the full value of their companies. 

The Pinecrest team has more than 75 years of combined experience in investment banking and has a depth of transactional knowledge and experience. Structured as a Merchant Bank, Pinecrest Capital Partners offers investment banking services and actively seeks opportunities to invest directly in companies seeking capital. 

Pinecrest's investment banking services include mergers and acquisitions, sell-side and buy-side; private capital sourcing; strategic advisory; and project finance. The industries Pinecrest serves include healthcare; industrials and manufacturing; transportation and logistics; energy, utilities and infrastructure; aerospace and defense; consumer products and services; technology and outsourced business services. Said another way, we are industry agnostic and serve clients across a wide array of industries. 

In addition to investment banking advisory services, Pinecrest also makes both control and non-control investments in select companies with strong business models, experienced management teams and unique value propositions.

Messrs. Kingsriter and Renkes are registered representatives of and Securities and Investment Banking Services are offered through BA Securities, LLC Member FINRA SIPC. Pinecrest Capital Partners and BA Securities, LLC are  separate and unaffiliated entities.

Title: Principal
Company Product and Services: innovation, customer advisory boards, design thinking, agile development, go-to-market, product evaluations, market research, product innovation, commercialization
Company Description:
The AND Group helps organizations derisk growth by grounding critical decisions in real customer perspective. 

Through structured, real-world engagement, we help leaders build conviction, stay aligned with the market, and deploy capital with confidence. 

Whether informing a specific decision or maintaining alignment over time, we bring real customer perspective into the process—helping organizations deploy capital more effectively, reduce wasted investment, and improve growth outcomes.

Two distinct ways to derisk growth: 

Customer Advisory Boards (CABs) provide organizations with an ongoing, structured way to stay closely connected to the people they serve. Through facilitated sessions with carefully selected customers, consumers, or stakeholders, leadership teams gain continuous insight into evolving needs, priorities, and perceptions. These boards go beyond feedback—they create a trusted forum for dialogue that strengthens relationships, informs strategy, and helps organizations make more confident, customer-centered decisions over time.

Customer-Informed Innovation helps organizations make smarter product and strategy decisions by engaging customers, consumers, and stakeholders early—before key investments are locked in. By observing and interacting with people in realistic contexts, companies gain a deeper understanding of how ideas perform in the real world. The result is not only sharper insight, but stronger internal alignment and clearer, customer-grounded narratives that support both decision-making and go-to-market success.

Title: Co-Founder
Company Description:
BKC Group is a market infrastructure and strategic advisory firm focused on institutional modernization and the next generation of systems shaping public finance and regulated markets.

The firm operates at the intersection of technology, market structure, and infrastructure strategy—helping institutions prepare for structural shifts affecting capital markets and the future architecture of public finance.

BKC Group is leading a consortium-driven effort focused on advancing next-generation infrastructure across issuance, settlement, operational coordination, and institutional workflows throughout the municipal market.

In parallel, BKC Group advises institutions through the Digital Infrastructure Readiness Initiative (DIRI), a structured engagement designed to help organizations evaluate modernization opportunities, infrastructure readiness, and the impact of emerging technologies.

Title: Founder
Company Description:
AcquireSuccess is an innovative partnership firm dedicated to empowering ambitious professionals to achieve business ownership without traditional barriers. Founded on the belief that entrepreneurship should be accessible, we specialize in co-acquiring established businesses through a collaborative model that minimizes risk and maximizes potential.

We believe in shared success. That's why we contribute 50% of the down payment, enabling partners to pursue larger, more profitable opportunities. Our team manages the upfront work: sourcing high-potential businesses, performing due diligence and financial analysis, and negotiating optimal terms.

Our support continues post-acquisition with strategic planning, operational improvements, growth strategies, and custom IT solutions—like apps, integrations, and tech upgrades—to boost efficiency and scale.

Partners become owner-operators, handling daily management with a competitive salary, plus shares in profits and equity. Backed by expertise in retail, logistics, and industrial growth, we provide the tools to turn acquisitions into thriving ventures.

Whether you're a seasoned executive or rising leader, we're here to realize your business dreams. Visit www.acquiresuccess.com to explore, or connect to discuss partnerships.

#BusinessAcquisition #Entrepreneurship #BusinessGrowth #MergersAndAcquisitions #OwnerOperator #AcquireSuccess

M&A Business Advisors are highly respected, experienced Advisors who are recognized as some of the top deal-makers in the industry. Our Advisors are considered “Influencers” and have been instrumental in improving the standards and ethics within the industry. Our Advisors are actively involved as industry association Presidents, industry association Board Members, Committee Chairs, Instructors as well as contributors to industry publications. 

Rest assure that when you are working with a M&A Business Advisor, you are working with the Leader in Business Sales & Acquisitions. Please refer to www.mabusinessadvisors.com for further details.

Title: Founder
Company Description:
Most SaaS companies don’t have a demand problem.
They have a pipeline problem.

Especially after a PE acquisition, when growth targets go up… and margin for error disappears.

That’s where I come in.

We help mid-to-large SaaS companies turn AI into a predictable growth engine without hiring bloated SDR teams or burning cash on tools that don’t talk to each other.

My background spans B2B marketing, sales systems, and GTM strategy, working with teams that needed results now, not “experiments.” Over time, I saw the same pattern repeat: great products, strong teams, but broken outbound and underperforming lead gen.

So I built a better system.

What We have helped teams achieve:
→ Consistent, qualified meetings booked weekly (not “leads,” actual conversations)
→ Lower CAC through AI-driven targeting, enrichment, and personalization
→ Faster ramp post–PE acquisition with repeatable outbound systems
→ Clean handoffs between marketing and sales (no more finger-pointing)

What I do today:
We run the AI Growth Engine a done-for-you B2B lead generation and appointment-setting system built specifically for SaaS.

It’s not just cold email.
It’s a full-stack outbound engine:
✅ AI-powered ICP & account selection
✅ Hyper-personalized messaging at scale
✅ Multi-channel outreach (email, LinkedIn, more)
✅ Deliverability, infrastructure, and reporting handled end-to-end
✅ Meetings booked directly onto your team’s calendar

This is a fit if you’re:
• A SaaS company doing $5M–$100M+ ARR
• Recently acquired by private equity and under pressure to grow fast
• Tired of underperforming SDR teams or agencies that sell “activity,” not outcomes

Next steps (pick one):
1️⃣ Follow me for practical insights on AI, outbound, and SaaS growth
2️⃣ DM me “AI GROWTH” if you want to see how the system works
3️⃣ Book a call via my profile if pipeline is a priority this quarter

Let’s turn AI into revenue not another internal project.

J. Daniel Samons
Founder / AgentX LLC.

Title: Managing Director
Company Product and Services: m&a, mergers and acquisitions, private placements, fundraising for growth companies, lbo, mbo, recapitalizations, refinancings, growth equity
Company Description:
Fairmount Partners is an independent investment bank focused on serving the needs of middle market and emerging growth companies.  We help our clients execute mergers & acquisitions (buy-side and sell-side) assignments; raise funds for growth; and design corporate development strategy.  Our clients include successful entrepreneurs, private equity sponsors, and global public companies, principally in the following verticals:

• Healthcare:  We serve a wide range of clients with a particular focus on outsourced clinical services, including clinical research organizations, lab testing service, healthcare & life sciences staffing, specialty pharmacy, diagnostic & imaging services and other related industries.  

• Industrial & Consumer:  With a keen focus on companies in the greater Delaware Valley area, we have deep experience executing transactions in a range of industries, including manufacturing; food & beverage products / services; distribution & logistics; metals and metals processing; plastics & packaging; and publishing & printing.  

• Consulting & Technology Services:  Supporting companies in the IT services, staffing, consulting, non-clinical healthcare services and other related services industries, Fairmount’s IT & business services practice focuses on the people-focused businesses, supporting business owners in a range of advisory and consultative services. 

• Technology:  We provide services across a wide span of technology businesses, including Enterprise and Cloud Software, Vertical Applications (e.g., energy, financial technology and healthcare IT), IoT and Industrial Technology, Digital Media, and Data Aggregation and Data Analytics.

With our expert advice and counsel, our clients have completed transactions in North and South America, Europe, Asia, Australia and the Middle East.

Fairmount Partners is a registered Broker Dealer, member FINRA (www.finra.org) and SIPC (www.sipc.org).

Title: Managing Director, Institutional Capital Markets
Company Product and Services: commercial real estate investing, real estate private equity, value add investing
Company Description:
Graceada Partners is a value-add real estate investor focused on apartments and industrial parks in secondary & tertiary markets of the Western US. With $1.6 trillion of industrial and multifamily real estate, these markets represent one of the last large inefficient sectors of real estate.

Founded in 2008, Graceada Partners employs a vertically integrated team of 60+ professionals who work collaboratively to source, capitalize, manage, renovate, lease, and sell properties. Graceada’s in-house expertise includes fund management, asset management, construction and property management.

The firm's leadership team averages over 18 years of experience adding value to real estate, collectively having managed over $10 billion in assets. Born in 2008 in the heart of the Global Financial Crisis, Graceada Partners is a seasoned expert with looming economic uncertainties, which has allowed the firm to turn market dislocation into successful investments. 

The Graceada Partners team lives by our core values: Positive, Caring & Humble; Owns the Mission; Does the Right Thing; Excellence in Execution; Wants to Win. Graceada Partners is a veteran-owned company that is proud to operate in historically underserved and under-invested regions.

Title: Managing Partner

We partner with owners, investors, and operators on hotel development, acquisitions, capital strategy, and asset repositioning across the Americas and EMEA — a comprehensive approach from sourcing and underwriting through to closing.

The firm draws on three decades of investment banking, private equity, and global hotel development across Belmond (LVMH) and Marriott International. We work across the full spectrum of luxury hospitality — grand hotels and branded residences, storied trains and river cruises, remote lodges, destination restaurants and spas, and private clubs — bringing institutional rigor and senior-led attention to every engagement.

Title: Managing Director
Company Product and Services: financial advisory, strategic advisory, underwriting, higher education, cultural institutions, not-for profit advisory, healthcare, environmental sustainability, social services, mergers & acquisitions
Company Description:
Founded in 1987 with offices in San Francisco and New York, Prager & Company (“Prager” or the “Firm”) is a leading independent investment bank offering world-class advisory and enterprise-building services to entities seeking to make a difference in the prosperity of people and society. Prager provides integrated, innovative, and comprehensive advisory and capital markets services to leading higher education institutions in the US and Canada; to social service and cultural institutions; to companies and social enterprises in healthcare, education technology and services, and sustainability; and to state and local government agencies.

The Firm is comprised of a diverse team of experienced professionals with extensive backgrounds in M&A, corporate and municipal finance, underwriting and financial administration. For over three decades we have been committed to delivering the best mission-driven outcomes for our clients. 

The Firm’s service offering has five components:
o	Financial Advisory, providing clients proprietary analytical and technical support as they assess and execute public debt and other forms of financings, including serving as Independent Registered Municipal Adviser (IRMA) in connection with municipal securities transactions.
o	Strategic Advisory, guiding clients through the process of originating and executing mergers, acquisitions, partnerships, and other structured transactions.
o	Private Capital, serving as agent in the private placement of equity and debt securities.
o	Strategic Consulting, advising clients to consider and assess key strategic, operational and structural issues and trends affecting their organization’s ability to achieve their mission and to prosper.
o	Underwriting, offering best execution and secondary market support where issuance of public municipal securities, taxable or tax-exempt, is deemed the preferred approach to raising capital.

Company Description:
New York Bay Capital is a New York-headquartered but international boutique investment banking firm experienced in providing financial advisory services in connection with mergers, acquisitions and capital raising initiatives primarily for foreign clients.  The Firm was established in 2015 to serve the growing demand for independent, partner-level advice by clients based outside the United States participating in the growing cross-border economy.  The Firm collaborates with financial affiliates and other partner firms to assist companies, from diverse industries, in structuring and financing transactions to achieve their overall corporate goals and financial objectives.

The Firm has representatives throughout the Western Hemisphere, including in Mexico, Canada, Central America, Colombia, Chile, Argentina, Brazil, Ecuador and Peru.  Recently the Firm has added one representative each in France, Switzerland, Spain, Israel and China.  The Firm collaborates with financial affiliates and partner firms to assist our clients in diverse industries in structuring cross-border transactions to achieve their companies’ overall corporate strategies and business objectives.

Our track record speaks for itself.  Please visit our webpage at http://www.newyorkbaycapital.com or follow us on Instagram @nybaycapital.

Title: Director
Company Product and Services: monetary policy, fiscal policy, legislative policy, foreign exchange markets, equity markets, fixed income markets, macroeconomic forecasting, commodities, political risk, china, india, federal reserve, japan, us congress
Company Description:
Observatory Group is an economic and political advisory firm that predicts monetary, fiscal and legislative policy, as well as other political and economic events of macroeconomic relevance.  Observatory Group’s analysis is designed to help buy-side investors stay ahead of the curve in understanding policy developments that could impact their investments.

Observatory Group covers the major policy institutions that are relevant to monetary and fiscal policy in the G3, China, India, and other geographies on an opportunistic basis. 

Offices:

New York
Washington, DC
Delhi
Tokyo
Bilbao
Beijing

Our History:
 
Observatory Group was founded in 2007 by John Chamberlin, Jane Hartley, Mary Rosenbaum and Jin Saito, who had all previously worked together at the G7 Group, a macro-economic research firm. Alan Blinder and Anita Volz Wien, who were partners at the G7 Group, joined Observatory Group as Vice Chairmen. In 2014, Jane Hartley became the United States Ambassador to France. John Chamberlin now runs the business from the New York office, along with Anita Volz Wien, who in 2014 was elevated to Chairman. Mary Rosenbaum and Jin Saito manage the firm’s research from the Washington, DC office.

Observatory Group initially covered the G3 (US, Eurozone, Japan) plus China. In early 2015, the firm added India to its geographic coverage area, and hired Surjit Bhalla, based in Delhi, who now covers India.

Title: Founder
Company Description:
Kansal Advisors is an AI enablement and strategy consultancy helping organizations turn AI ambition into measurable results.

We work at the intersection of technology and execution — supporting companies, leadership teams, and private equity-backed businesses that have a vision for AI but need the structure, expertise, and hands-on support to make it real.

Our work spans three areas:

AI Readiness Assessments — diagnosing where your organization stands across people, process, data, and technology, and identifying your highest-value AI opportunities.

AI Strategy & Roadmaps — building prioritized, actionable plans that connect AI investments to business outcomes. Built for execution, not just presentation.

Implementation & Enablement — embedded support through deployment, adoption, and scale — with a focus on change management and building lasting internal capability.

We specialize in the people and organizational side of AI transformation — the part most technology firms overlook. Our clients range from growth-stage companies to enterprise organizations and private equity portfolio companies navigating AI-driven value creation.

Based in Atlanta. Working nationally.

Company Description:
CIS is a national leader in the development and implementation of public infrastructure solutions. Our focus is on improving the nation’s infrastructure via economic development through local investment, and local capacity building, reduced public risk, and increased community investment and buy-in.  Where needed, we can also provide access to capital needed to undertake and successfully complete a project. 

CIS also offers related advisory expertise and services on topics such as nature-based solutions, climate resilience and disaster prevention, environmental finance, water affordability, and public trust.

CIS currently has hundreds of millions of dollars worth of program backlog in its near-term pipeline.

Title: Organizational Effectiveness Partner
Company Product and Services: organizational design, operating model development, organizational effectiveness, leadership team effectiveness, role clarity, decision frameworks, executive advisory, change & transformation, m&a integration, ai-enabled organizational diagnosis, strategic people advisory
Company Description:
Orsini | Effective by Design is an organizational effectiveness advisory practice founded by Renée Orsini.

We partner with leadership teams to identify the friction points that slow execution, blur accountability, and keep strong organizations from performing at their full potential — then design operating models and align structure, roles, decision-making, and capabilities to drive real change.

What presents as an execution gap, a missed goal, or an interpersonal conflict is usually a deeper issue in how the organization is designed or how decisions are made. We see those structural issues early — often before they are broadly recognized — and help leadership teams act before they become costly.

Our work spans:
▸ Organizational design and operating model development
▸ Role clarity and decision framework redesign
▸ Leadership team alignment and effectiveness
▸ Founder-led to scaled operating model transition
▸ M&A integration and high-stakes organizational transition
▸ Practical AI-enabled diagnosis — surfacing patterns, pressure-testing workflows, and improving how work gets done

We work with growth-stage and mid-size companies at meaningful inflection points — scaling, acquisition, leadership transition, or a moment when leadership knows something is not working and is ready to make the decisions required to fix it.

Engagements are retained and strategic. We keep a small number of active clients for exactly that reason.

Title: Principal
Company Description:
SVH Capital: Helping You Grow, Buy, or Sell a Business

At SVH Capital, we help business owners and shareholders grow their companies, sell them, or buy new ones. We use smart technology and personal support to make complex deals easier and more successful.

What We Do:

1. Mergers & Acquisitions (M&A):
We guide you through the process of selling your business for the best value or buying other companies to help you grow.

2. Growth Capital:

Need funding to grow? We connect you with investors who can provide capital through equity or debt.

3. Secondary Transactions:

Want to take some money off the table? We help founders and partners sell shares or provide other alternatives to unlock capital.

Why Work With SVH Capital?

We’re not your traditional investment bank. SVH Capital combines deep knowledge with modern tools to deliver results.

•	Based in Silicon Valley with global connections
•	Experience with complex deals across many industries
•	Focused on getting the best outcome for you

Our Promise to You:

We take pride in being honest, smart, and always putting our clients first.

•	Strategies built around your goals
•	Clear advice with no hidden fees
•	Expert insights backed by real data
•	Always adapting to what’s next in the market

“Our mission is to give business owners smarter strategies, faster execution, and better results from a partner they can trust.”

Shawn Flynn
Managing Director, SVH Capital

Title: Founder
Company Description:
EOMGTZ is an operator-led value creation firm for mid-market businesses. We embed fractional executives, CFOs, COOs, Heads of Strategy, Heads of Data, into companies between $15M and $200M in revenue, at the inflection points where the existing leadership bench can't carry the next chapter alone.
The firm is built around an unusual premise: the people running engagements have actually run businesses. Our operators have served as Chief of Staffs, Heads of IT, CFOs, VPs of Finance, CMOs, and other executive positions inside Private Equity Backed Companies, and Fortune 100 companies. They have integrated acquired companies, rebuilt data architectures, run operating reviews, and made the trade-offs that only show up when you're the one accountable for the result.

We work with three audiences: PE-backed portfolio companies in active hold periods, founder-led businesses preparing for institutional capital or sale, and operating companies between $15M and $200M that need senior leadership without committing to a full-time hire. Engagements are scoped, named, and outcome-owned. We are not a staffing firm and we are not a strategy consultancy. We are operators who fill seats.

Emory's operations are local, regional, national, and international. We serve a wide variety of industries, including distribution, food, manufacturing, service and technology companies.

Title: Co-Founder
Company Description:
Rainier Associates is a distinguished buy-side advisory firm headquartered in Olympia, WA, dedicated to delivering unparalleled investment opportunities through proprietary, off-market deals. Our inception, deeply rooted in a legacy that spans over 15 years with our sister company, reflects our enduring commitment to excellence and innovation in the investment advisory sector. In 2024, we embarked on a strategic journey to establish Rainier Associates, aiming to cater more specifically to the buy-side by leveraging our expertise in sourcing and structuring unique investment opportunities that drive value and growth.

Our foundation is built on the extensive experience and visionary leadership of our team, spearheaded by industry veterans who have demonstrated a profound ability to navigate the complex landscape of investments. With a proven track record, our operations encompass a broad spectrum of industries, including Technology and Software, Healthcare, Business Services, Consumer Goods and Retail, Financial Services, Energy and Utilities, Industrial and Manufacturing, Education and Training, Infrastructure and Environmental Services, and Food and Beverage. This diversity allows us to tailor our strategies to meet the specific needs and objectives of our clients, ensuring bespoke solutions that align with their long-term goals.

At Rainier Associates, we pride ourselves on a strategic approach to buy-side advisory, where we not only focus on identifying opportunities but also on understanding the intricacies that define and drive different sectors. Our expertise extends to working with a wide range of acquiring entities, from Fortune 500 corporate strategics, family offices, independent sponsors and traditional private equity firms.

Title: Director
Company Product and Services: mergers and acquisitions, middle market, investment banking, advisory, consulting, divestiture, exit planning
Company Description:
RLS Associates provides professional merger, acquisition and divestiture consulting services – as well as other management and growth capital consulting services – to companies and individuals seeking to buy or sell businesses or to grow and improve their existing enterprise. 

Active since 1986, the firm’s team of experienced professionals has earned a premier reputation in the region for ethical and effective intermediary and consulting services.  

RLS focuses on companies with Revenues of $2 to $100 million in manufacturing, distribution, services and technology related businesses.  Its services and expertise help business owners develop and implement business continuation and exit strategies that fit with their personal goals. It assists business buyers, whether corporations seeking strategic acquisitions or entrepreneurs and private equity funds pursuing investment opportunities, in locating and negotiating profitable transactions.

Our approach is designed to: 

1.	Protect confidentiality for clients throughout the process.
2.	Locate the right buyers, sellers and/or investors. 
3.	Maximize client economic and strategic benefit. 
4.	Minimize the time clients divert from their business, while maintaining client control. 
5.	Provide objective, executive level strategic advice and insights.

Company Description:
Interlochen provides CFOs and controllers on an outsourced or project basis. Engagements can be structured on an  interim, fractional, or part-time basis depending upon your requirements. Interlochen can also help recruit your full-time CFO or controller.

Since 2000, Interlochen has placed proven CFOs and controllers with over 250 small and mid-sized companies. Most of these businesses were facing a critical transition. Examples include: ownership or management changes, rapid growth or decline, M&A, restructuring, and refinancing. Interlochen interim CFOs average nearly 30 years of “C level“ experience. Their financial and operational experience in a broad array of industries, knowledge of the debt and equity markets, combined with excellent leadership and teambuilding skills means that they can begin to add value on Day One.

Interlochen Group is based in Atlanta and primarily services the Southeastern US. Past clients are always our best referral source. However, many clients are referred through our network of trusted professional service providers, which includes: accountants, attorneys, consultants, lenders, investment bankers, and private equity groups.

In 2012, Interlochen joined FINACA (Finance and Accounting Consultants Association). FINACA is a nationwide network of independent finance and accounting consulting firms focused on delivering exceptional client service. This affiliation enables Interlochen to provide you with experienced accounting and finance professionals from coast to coast.

Company Description: For over 100 years, Mergent, Inc. has been a leading provider of business and financial information on public and private companies globally. Company Description:
Corporate Valuation Advisors, Inc. ("CVA"​) is a full service valuation and financial advisory firm, celebrating 35+ years of providing valuations of business enterprises, tangible assets, and intangible assets and advising our clients on various tax and financial reporting issues. 

Whether a business enterprise, a single share of stock, a stock warrant, a stock option, improved real estate, machinery and equipment or an intangible asset, certain assumptions are inherent in the estimation of value. Since assumptions affect value, then facts must support the basis of those assumptions to the greatest extent possible. 

We pride ourselves on being a fact-based organization. We readily acknowledge the uncompromising link between facts and supportable assumptions and the vulnerability of any value estimate bred from the impulsive journey of many into the unrepentant world of unsupported assumptions. It is for this reason that we rely upon extensive market research as well as industry data and trends to establish the facts in order to formulate and support the assumptions which underlie the development of our value estimate. To do otherwise only allows for the square peg of value to become hopelessly lodged in the round hole of controversy and dispute. 

Thank you for following Corporate Valuation Advisors.

Company Description:
CPP is focused on providing strategic and operational consulting services to small and middle market businesses located in the Northeast US. Underpinning our offering is the belief that every business would benefit from a periodic “health assessment” leading to:
•	Identification/quantification of current/anticipated performance issues.
•	Identification of changes necessary to reach and sustain performance improvement.
•	Assessment of the ability of client’s in-house resources to execute necessary changes.
An assessment might be undertaken on a business-wide basis or only for a particular functional area/areas. 

Large firms typically conduct these sorts of assessments on a continuous basis, but smaller firms often do not have the staffing, extent of knowledge, or available time required to undertake such in-depth reviews.

CPP is a seasoned and cohesive team of professionals with leadership experience in planning, business/market development, finance, operations, offering development, capital investment, restructuring, and mergers, acquisitions and dispositions. With an average of 25 years of diverse industry and functional experience, CPP Principals actively manage each engagement. They are particularly adept at crafting practical, client-sensitive solutions to performance and growth challenges and at leading client teams in the implementation of changes to resolve performance issues and to fully realize the benefits to be gained from implementing those changes.

Believing that the only way to deliver lasting change is for consultants to work with, not just for, the client, Principals engage relevant client personnel at all phases of a project from situation assessment to implementation.

Post implementation, clients typically ask CPP to conduct periodic progress reviews to confirm that actual results meet or exceed projected results and as appropriate, to recommend corrective actions.

Contact us to arrange an exploratory meeting to see if CPP is right for you.

Title: Co-Founder
Company Product and Services: co-investing, search fund investing, smb acquisitions, alternative investments, private equity, investor relations, deal syndication, portfolio management
Company Description:
SMB Investor Network is the co-investment platform for Search Fund Ventures, connecting accredited investors with deal-by-deal opportunities in lower-market business acquisitions.

We give qualified investors direct access to the same deals our fund invests in — with $10K minimums and full transparency into every opportunity.

What investors get:
• Deal-by-deal co-investment access alongside Search Fund Ventures
• Professional underwriting with conservative assumptions and margin of safety
• Full deal memos, financial models, and due diligence summaries
• Ongoing portfolio updates and LP communication
• Access to our quarterly Closed Deals Reports with proprietary market data

The search fund model has historically produced 3–5x returns with a 69% profitability rate (Stanford, 2024). Our approach is portfolio-based — targeting 20–30 companies per vintage to capture upside while managing idiosyncratic risk.

We believe lower-market investing shouldn't require $100K+ minimums or institutional connections. It should be transparent, data-driven, and accessible to qualified investors who want exposure to a historically attractive asset class.

smbinvestornetwork.com

Title: Managing Partner
Company Product and Services: due diligence, product stategy, fda, ce, regulatory strategy, product development, human factors, market access, market research, business development, product management, quality management, portfolio strategy, medical devices, software-as-a-medical device, medical supply chain, digital health, ambulatory care, population health, mixed reality
Company Description:
At Synthesize, we are on a mission to enable the future of health by helping builders and backers build transformative ventures that improve human health. We come in strategic consultants to help solve specific problems or as Fractional Cross-functional team to help with product and venture building.  

Building ventures in healthcare is hard, riddled with regulations and principal-agent effects. 

In the next 10 years, the collision between the worlds of ambulatory, allied and home care, and fusion of devices, AI, and digital health software will lead to a paradigm shift in managing human health and disease. We also understand the high risk that ventures in health technology come with. To address that we leverage our cross-functional expertise, and highly experienced ecosystem of vetted partners in clinical, testing & manufacturing, operational, policy & regulatory, and commercialization to de-risk ventures on technology, scaling, and commercialization. 

Some of our exciting work has been in Software as Medical Devices, Use of Mixed Reality in Diagnostics and Therapeutics, Novel minimally invasive surgical products, and implantable devices. 

For Builders we offer, Product, Regulatory, Market Access and Business Strategy consulting. We work with HealthTech startups and companies with new product initiatives.

For Backers we offer, Investment Diligence, M&A Advisory and Technology Transfer for Corporate M&A/Venture arms. We work with VC & PE firms, Family Offices and Angel Investors.

For Provider businesses/health systems - we help choose best medical devices, digital health and IT technologies that best fit their organization.

For Universities and Research Organization - we facilitate technology transfer with partners in our ecosystem.

Lastly, we invest back in our own R&D to nurture a couple of exciting projects.

Title: Founder
Company Product and Services: proprietary deal flow, m&a advisory, private equity deal sourcing, deal origination, business brokerage, exit planning, business valuation, mergers and acquisitions, pe deal flow infrastructure,  buy-side deal sourcing, consent-based lead generation, lower middle market m&a, sell-side advisory, business succession planning, platform deal origination, inbound deal flow, hvac business valuation, home care m&a, service business m&a, transaction advisory
Company Description:
Second Chair Advisory is a buy-side search firm delivering retained acquisition sourcing for PE firms, family offices, and independent sponsors deploying capital in home services and healthcare services.

We combine three sourcing layers to deliver qualified acquisition targets that match your buy box:

Proprietary Inbound. Our Exit Lab platform network generates direct signals from business owners who are considering a transition but have not yet engaged an intermediary. These are pre-market opportunities that do not appear on broker listings or auction processes.

Targeted Outbound. AI-native prospecting systems identify, enrich, and tier potential targets by acquisition readiness. We build and execute direct outreach campaigns against your specific criteria.

Referral and Network. Active relationships with M&A intermediaries and industry operators who surface opportunities before they are broadly marketed.
The result is a search practice with a structural sourcing advantage. Our clients receive qualified targets that their internal teams and competing buyers cannot access through traditional channels.

Engagement structures are retained search with 90-day cycles. We work with a limited number of concurrent clients to maintain depth and exclusivity across geographies and verticals.

Active coverage: HVAC, plumbing, roofing, home care, home health, hospice, assisted living, and adjacent categories across Texas, Oklahoma, Louisiana, Arizona, Idaho, Georgia, Utah, and the Southeast.

Title: Managing Partner
Company Product and Services: strategic advisory, operations management, oil & gas, executive facilitation, organizational design, project execution, energy sector consulting, operational clarity, leadership alignment, program management
Company Description:
Thompson Advisory Partners (TAP) is a strategic advisory firm built for the Oil & Gas sector. We partner with executive teams to drive operational clarity, build cross-functional alignment, and execute with discipline — from the C-suite to the field.

Unlike traditional consulting firms, TAP operates from the inside. We embed with your leadership team, learn your operating environment, and work alongside your people to solve complex operational challenges. Our approach is direct, focused, and built around outcomes.

TAP's three core service areas reflect how real operational challenges present themselves in the field: strategic operations advisory, project and program execution, and organizational clarity. Each engagement is structured around your timeline, your constraints, and your definition of success.

We serve upstream E&P operators, midstream companies, and private equity-backed energy firms that need a proven operating partner. Our clients choose TAP when they need a partner who has operated inside the industry, can think outside the box, understands the pace and complexity of energy operations, and can drive results.

Thompson Advisory Partners. Clarity. Alignment. Execution.

Title: Founder & Managing Partner
Company Product and Services: strategic planning, business growth, organizational culture, organizational effectiveness, executive retreats, social ventures, scaling up, leadership, team alignment, ngos, leadership development, leadership team facilitation, b corporations, team building, values, operating models, stakeholder engagement
Company Description:
Telescope resolves misalignment through critical conversations, pivotal decisions, and the necessary changes that follow — transforming teams to act clearly, quickly, and together.

Strategic Clarity: the full arc from purpose and foundation through vision, priorities, and quarterly execution. Stakeholder interviews surface what the data doesn't show. Facilitated sessions produce decisions, not discussion. Ongoing advisory carries strategy from the room into the organization.

Operational Alignment: aligning structure, roles, and resources to the strategy; redesigning systems, cadences, and practices for its execution. 

Business and Operational Planning: the analytical depth and organizational breadth a specific inflection point demands. Intensive work for pivots, new ventures, and capability sequencing.

Interim COO / Chief of Staff:  experienced on-site operational leadership, available on short notice for executive transitions, execution gaps, and infrastructure buildout.

Telescope works with organizations at inflection points — $10M to $150M — through introductions from capital partners, prior clients, and trusted networks.

What matters gets said. What's decided gets done.

Title: Principal
Company Product and Services: aerospace & defense, industrial products & services, business services
Company Description:
McNally Capital is a private equity firm based in Chicago, Illinois. The Firm is currently investing out of its committed buyout fund, McNally Capital Fund III, LP.

The Firm pursues thesis-driven buyout investments in the lower middle market across two primary industries: Aerospace & Defense and Industrial Technology & Services. McNally Capital seeks to apply its hands-on experience, institutional capabilities, and proprietary value creation framework to its portfolio companies to benefit management teams in their next phase of growth and build value for McNally Capital’s investors.
 
Ward McNally, Co-CEO & Managing Partner, founded the firm in 2008. He is a sixth-generation member of the McNally family, which proudly owned and operated Rand McNally & Company for nearly 150 years until its sale in 1997. We believe these roots provide a deep appreciation of building and scaling companies that shape industries and endure for generations. This experience drove the Firm to invest capital into founder-, family-, and management-led businesses in the lower middle market on behalf of investors, with the goal of preserving legacies while capitalizing on possibilities.
 
Today, the Firm's team is dedicated to navigating transformation and growth for its portfolio companies through a rigorous, repeatable, and scalable value creation process.

Title: Partner
Company Product and Services: executive onboarding and transition acceleration, team acceleration, private equity, ceo onboarding, mergers & acquisitions
Company Description:
When leadership changes, results can’t wait.
PrimeGenesis helps C-suite executives, Private Equity firms, and HR leaders accelerate success during high-stakes transitions. Through our proven 100-Day Action Plan and hands-on support, we help new leaders and teams deliver better results faster, getting done in 100 days what normally takes 6 to 12 months.

Since 2003, PrimeGenesis has been the trusted authority in executive and team onboarding. Our partners are former operating executives and organizational development experts who bring real-world experience, practical insight, and decades of results guiding leaders through complex transitions.

Our proprietary tools and frameworks, featured in The New Leader’s 100-Day Action Plan, Onboarding, The Merger & Acquisition Leaders Playbook, and Point of Inflection, are relied on by Fortune 500 companies and leading Private Equity firms worldwide. 

Clients trust us to reduce risk, create alignment, and build momentum across C-suite transitions, mergers and acquisitions, and team integrations. That’s why our top clients have partnered with PrimeGenesis more than 200 times.

We make executive onboarding success the rule, not the exception.
Learn more at www.PrimeGenesis.com.

Company Description:
FRsix is a privately-held business focused on the investment into, and management of, cornerstone businesses across a variety of industries. FRsix excels in the development of operational efficiencies and business growth with a dedication to people, process and results.

Owned businesses include security operations, leadership and team development, business consulting, and hospitality industry mergers & acquisitions.

Title: Senior Managing Director, Head of Institutional Sales
Company Product and Services: investment management
Company Description:
M.D. Sass is an independent asset management firm that has been creating highly differentiated investment strategies for over 50 years.  We manage portfolios directly and also partner with early stage managers to build new and innovative investment businesses.  We take pride in identifying undiscovered and misperceived opportunities through rigorous research.  The firm was founded in March of 1972 on four key principles:

•   Our belief that many attractive investment opportunities exist outside the bounds of the conventional wisdom of the moment
•   Our willingness to conduct rigorous research to find and capture potential rewards and identify, measure and control risks
•   Our dedication to providing clients with service and solutions tailored to their specific circumstances, objectives and risk tolerances
•   Our commitment to invest our own capital alongside that of our clients

Since then, we have started and grown 34 different investment businesses across a wide array of strategies.  Today, the firm manages a range of equity, fixed income and alternative strategies for public funds, corporations, Taft-Hartley funds, endowments/foundations, private banks, registered investment advisors, and individuals.

Title: Managing Partner | Global Capital Architect,  Fractional CFO, Tokenization and StableCoin Strategies
Company Description:
Strategic Capital. Operational Clarity. Global Execution.

Ameen & Co. is a modern merchant-style advisory firm focused on unlocking capital, restoring operational clarity, and scaling ventures across complex markets. We serve as strategic partners to founders, sponsors, and sovereign-aligned entities navigating multi-party international deals.

Our work blends capital architecture, operational leadership, and international execution—supporting clients where precision, discretion, and complexity meet. From early-stage growth to special situations, we operate as embedded advisors and fractional CFOs, bringing institutional discipline to entrepreneurial environments.

WHAT WE DO
🔹 Capital Strategy & Structuring – Craft financial roadmaps, investor materials, and capital stack models to support private market engagement and institutional alignment.
🔹 Fractional CFO & Turnaround – Restore control through cash visibility, forecasting discipline, and operational restructuring—especially in moments of change or recovery.
🔹 Multi-Party International Transactions – Navigate trade, jurisdictional complexity, and execution readiness across Africa, Asia, the Gulf, and LATAM.
🔹 Tokenization & Digital Infrastructure – Support the design of real-world asset tokenization, digital finance platforms, and FX-stabilized investment models.

We don’t raise capital or sell securities. Instead, we architect strategy, structure, and execution support—working behind the scenes to help clients move with confidence, clarity, and control.

If you’re navigating complexity and need a trusted hand at the financial and operational helm, we’re here to help you build it, fix it, and grow it.

Title: Principal
Company Description:
424 Capital makes control equity investments in lower middle market companies within B2B Tech-Enabled Services. We work in partnership with founders and management teams to accelerate and scale for long-term growth. Focusing primarily on investments in North America, we invest in companies that are profitable, growing, and that align with our values of honesty, integrity and impact.  At 13 full-time investment professionals, remaining small and nimble is important to our strategy and allows us to work shoulder-to-shoulder with our portfolio company management teams to build dominant market leaders. Together, our team has invested in a total of 70 control transactions; at 3-5 new platform investments a year, we are selective in the investments we make.

Success to us requires portfolio companies achieving results on a level of scale that even they didn’t imagine was possible. Over many years of investing and operating together, we have experienced firsthand the benefit of rolling up our sleeves and working with our portfolio company founders and CEOs whenever and wherever they need us. We help tailor a unique strategy for each portfolio company that involves accelerated revenue velocity, product and service line extensions, and a scale that creates a true industry-changing market leader. Energized, engaged and goal oriented, we have a long successful history of applying our vision to help build great businesses.

RNP advises clients on acquisitions and divestitures, growth strategies, and operational improvements.  RNP’s solutions are based on the proven practices of today’s top performing companies and are tailored to fit the needs of the individual client. The RNP team has 50+ years of proven experience in Fortune 500 and private equity firms and has produced over $900M of new growth and closed over $500M in M&A deals.

•	Partnering with clients and assessing opportunities

Growth Strategies
RNP develops high performing commercial organizations through the application of proven sales and marketing processes which enable clients to realize growth objectives faster.  With long-term planning and strategy deployment, these high performing teams deliver breakthrough growth for the organization.  

Operational Improvements
RNP’s clients overcome costly and complex operational challenges through the application of lean value stream principles.  Targeted workshops produce standard work procedures that reduce cost, improve efficiency, increase operating cash flow, and enhance quality and delivery.

Title: Managing Director, Head of Sales
Company Product and Services: information technology investment banking, research, corporate finance, m&a advisory
Company Description:
Pacific Crest Securities was acquired by KeyBanc Capital Markets to better serve the technology sector. Stay connected to our experts and industry insights by following the KeyBanc Capital Markets page.

Pacific Crest was the premier investment bank for technology. The firm operated at the leading edge, where global connectivity is fueling an unprecedented expansion cycle. The firm applied its knowledge of the drivers of value creation and global network of relationships to technology's high-growth sectors, such as global internet, mobility, SaaS, cloud and big data, next gen infrastructure, and energy and industrial technology. As a result, Pacific Crest's clients — technology's foremost institutional investors and market leading companies — relied on it to achieve superior returns and gain competitive advantage from the seismic shifts occurring in technology.

In July 2014, KeyCorp's investment banking unit, KeyBanc Capital Markets (KBCM), announced its intention to acquire Pacific Crest and Pacific Crest was merged into KBCM in 2015.

At the time of the acquisition, Pacific Crest Securities had 170 employees located in offices in Portland, Oregon, Boston, New York, San Francisco, Stamford, Connecticut and international offices in Beijing, London as well as the Pacific Epoch office in Shanghai.

As a result, Pacific Crest's clients — technology's foremost institutional investors and market leading companies — relied on it to achieve superior returns and gain competitive advantage from the seismic shifts occurring in technology.

In July 2014, KeyCorp's investment banking unit, KeyBanc Capital Markets (KBCM), announced its intention to acquire Pacific Crest and Pacific Crest was merged into KBCM in 2015.

At the time of the acquisition, Pacific Crest Securities had 170 employees located in offices in Portland, Oregon, Boston, New York, San Francisco, Stamford, Connecticut and international offices in Beijing, London as well as the Pacific Epoch office in Shanghai.

Company Description:
FIC Partners Management, LP ("FIC") is an investment firm, dedicated to thoughtful infrastructure investing in energy and we are accelerating the development of strategic assets that serve societal utility needs. FIC was born from a vision to create a leading middle-market infrastructure firm. We seek to leverage our team's experiences and relationships to invest in infrastructure serving the power and power-use value chains, which we believe are supported by multi-decade tailwinds. ​

​Our focus is on robust, high-quality infrastructure assets that are critical or strategically positioned for resiliency across cycles in the following sectors: gas transmission, downstream, utilities, renewables & de-carbonization and data / telecom. 

We partner with management teams and provide capital to support the growth, optimization and enhancement of critical infrastructure assets at all phases of the development lifecycle: greenfield, brownfield, expansion and developed. 

FIC believes in an “all-of-the-above" energy future where fossil fuels, renewables and electrification co-exist. We seek investments that IMPROVE existing energy infrastructure and value chains, SUPPORT a smooth energy transition, or REDUCE the carbon intensity of energy used today.
