Company Description:
Fidus Capital is the external management company of Fidus Investment Corporation (www.fdus.com), a publicly traded (NASDAQ: FDUS) business development company (“BDC”) with over $900 million of assets under management. The Partners of Fidus Capital possess on average over 25 years of experience investing in, lending to, and advising lower middle market companies across a broad spectrum of industries. Fidus’ flexible investment capabilities enable it to invest throughout the capital structure, with targeted investments consisting of senior secured / unitranche debt, mezzanine / subordinated debt, and equity securities. Fidus also manages a lower middle market private equity fund targeting equity co-investments of $2 million to $7 million. Since 2007, Fidus has successfully invested more than $2 billion of capital in more than 150 founder-owned and private equity-backed companies and currently oversees more than 75 portfolio company investments.

Fidus typically seeks to invest between $10 million to $100 million in companies with the following characteristics:

- EBITDA greater than $5 million
- Cash flow, recurring revenue-based and asset-backed credit facilities
- Backed by financial sponsors, management teams, or business owners
- Target Industry Focuses: Aerospace & Defense, Business Services, Consumer 
   Products, Energy Services, Healthcare Products, Industrial, Infrastructure, Niche Manufacturing, Transportation & Logistics, Software & Tech-Enabled Services and Value-Added Distribution.

Fidus has offices in Chicago, Charlotte, and New York.

Title: Director Infrastructure Services
Company Description:
WHO WE ARE
The SOMOS Group is a group of multidisciplinary think-and-do professionals dedicated to partnering with public- and private-sector clients to build and sustain community resilience, promote economic development, increase social equity and accelerate prosperity.  As a BIPOC-owned business, SOMOS’ work is aligned with broader equity goals and advancing the value and expertise of people and communities that have  been historically underserved and undervalued.  Innovating is our business.

OUR VALUES
SOMOS was founded on the premise that clients need results-oriented solutions benefiting from professionals that are experts at harnessing multiple disciplines and expertise: qualitative and quantitative research, strategic planning, entitlements and permitting, communications, outreach and engagement, labor relations and government advocacy. Effective solutions to complex, systemic challenges require innovative services, cross-disciplinary expertise as well as experience in consulting with a broad range of stakeholders and communities.

WHAT WE DO
SOMOS professionals work with clients to:

● Research and prepare studies for smart cities aimed at innovating and attracting public-private investments, reforming systems for permitting and  regulating development, and improving access to resources.
Plan, design and manage successful long range, master planning and land  use policies.
● Investigate best and highest use of land for government and private owners across industries and land types to guide policy, community benefits and site planning.
● Oversee major, complex planning and development projects for government and institutions.
● Guide, design and implement competitive bid programs and initiatives consulting closely with businesses, property owners, and residents.

Title: Co-Founder & CTO
Company Description:
Deal Scout is an AI platform purpose-built for private markets investment managers — connecting the full investment lifecycle, from first document to final LP statement, on a single connected record.

Our AI Investment Analyst processes deal documents, extracts financial data with full source citation, builds LBO and DCF models from confirmed inputs, and produces IC memos traceable to the underlying data — in a fraction of the time traditionally required. Every figure links to the exact page it came from. Every model uses deterministic financial mathematics, not AI estimation.

When capital is deployed, our AI Investment Operating System takes over — managing fund accounting, NAV calculation, covenant monitoring, capital calls, wire processing, and LP reporting from the same record that began at first diligence. No re-entry. No data loss at the handoff. Every position, every distribution, every LP statement traceable back to the original investment analysis.

Deal Scout serves investment managers across every private markets asset class — private equity, private credit, real assets, infrastructure, secondaries, co-investments, and fund-of-funds — and is designed to scale from emerging managers and family offices through to established funds. Institutional infrastructure, accessible to any team that demands it.

Security and governance are built into the architecture from the ground up. Customer deal data is never used for AI model training, and every process is designed to meet the standards that institutional investors and their advisers expect.

Title: Founder & Principal

Company Description:
The greatest lie in business is that you must trade kindness for results.

Yet, empathetic leaders often find themselves navigating broken systems that push them to compromise their values. They burn out, their teams disengage, and the business ultimately pays the Misalignment Tax.

Whether you are building a new leadership bench in a high-change environment or fighting to insulate your team from top-down strategic swirl, odds are you are paying this tax. 

You deserve better scaffolding.

My career began in Computer Science and Human-Computer Interaction, but I quickly realized the most persistent bugs in any operating model aren't in the code - they are in the very human ecosystem.

At Capital One, I evolved from a Go-To-Market Design Director to leading Talent & Culture for a 700-person division. Today, as Founder & Principal Architect at Knickmeyer Strategic Facilitation, I use that architectural rigor to help you cut through the messiness of leadership.

There is no one-size-fits-all fix for systemic friction. I partner with executive teams through a phased, diagnostic-first approach using three core architectural pathways:

🔍 TEAM ALIGNMENT DIAGNOSTICS: Internal listening tours only reveal part of the picture. Through a two-week surgical deep-dive, we pinpoint exactly where the friction lives and build the definitive roadmap to reclaim your momentum

⚡️ STRATEGIC CATALYSTS: We move your leadership from diagnosis to structural design. Catalyst outcomes are tailored to your exact friction points

🤝 ADVISORY RETAINERS: Transformation takes more than one off-site to stick. I provide continuous, 3-to-6-month advisory guidance as you implement your new infrastructure design

Ultimately, our greatest corporate successes are the relationships we build. My entire approach is anchored in this truth, because trust and alignment are the bedrock upon which true innovation is built.

Ready to reclaim your values and your momentum?

Visit www.knickmeyer.net to learn more.

Title: Founder and Managing Partner
Company Description:
We provide executive advisory, consulting, and capital partnership for leaders building businesses that last.

Most business advice is about getting bigger. We help businesses get stronger. The companies that achieve their founders' vision aren't always the ones that grew the fastest — they're the ones that built the right foundation and grew from it.

Your business model may work. Your operating model might not. Strategic ambiguity, team misalignment, execution drag, financial vulnerability, founder dependence — these are the predictable scaling problems we help leaders solve before they show up.

TWO WAYS WE WORK

Consulting — Your Strategic Wingman.
Fixed-fee and retained engagements that install the systems, clarity, and discipline that make businesses resilient. Three tracks: AI Enablement, Resilience Building, and Exit Readiness. Our V Method pairs proven business frameworks with targeted AI integration — frameworks without AI are slow; AI without the foundation is noise.

Partnership — Your Operating Wingman.
Capital and operating partnership for resilient service businesses. We invest in and build alongside operators through seed investments, acquisitions, and long-hold partnerships. An operator's posture, not a financier's.

Grounded in 25+ years of operator experience: 7× Inc. 5000, 5× Inc. Best Places to Work, 5 acquisitions led, 3 successful exits.

We come alongside founders, CEOs, Presidents, and COOs in professional services, technology and SaaS, marketing agencies, skilled trades, and field-operating businesses. The through-line is the same: address the drag, install the foundation, help you fly in formation.

If you're building something meant to last, let's talk.

Title: President & Founder
Company Product and Services: mergers & acquisitions, business appraisals, retained acquisition search, representation to sell businesses, consulting to build value, business valuation, business brokerage
Company Description:
Most business owners don’t lose value when they sell… 
They lose it long before the process even begins. 

Not because the business isn’t good - 
but because it isn’t positioned, prepared, or timed correctly. 

At Horizon M&A Advisors, we work with lower middle-market ($1M-$100M) founders and business owners to prepare, position, and sell their companies for maximum value - not just close deals.

 Because in M&A, outcomes aren’t decided at the negotiating table. They’re decided months - sometimes years - before a buyer ever shows up. 

Many owners: 

Enter the market too late. 
Misjudge what buyers actually value. 
Lose leverage before negotiations even begin. 

By the time they realize it… 
they’re already negotiating from a weaker position. 

We help change that. 

For Business Owners (Sell-Side)

• Understand what your business is truly worth - beyond surface-level valuations. 
• Position your business so buyers compete, not negotiate down.
• Create competitive tension to drive stronger outcomes.
• Structure deals that protect value - not quietly give it away. 
• Navigate negotiations and due diligence with confidence. 
• Maintain strict confidentiality throughout.

For Buyers & Investors 

We work with select acquirers to identify and execute high-quality opportunities with strategic fit and long-term value. 

Our approach is built on confidentiality, integrity, strategic execution, and a client-first mindset - but more importantly, on telling clients what they need to hear, not just what they want to hear. 

With 30+ years of combined experience, 500+ deals closed, and $1B+ in transaction value, we’ve seen firsthand what separates strong exits from disappointing ones. 

If you’re considering a sale in the next 1-3 years, it’s worth understanding how a buyer would value your business today. 

Happy to share what that might look like for your situation - feel free to reach out directly at greg@horizonmaa.com or DM us.

Title: Managing Director, Operations and Policy
Company Product and Services: investment management, nature-based solutions, ecological restoration, mitigation banking
Company Description:
Founded in 2006, Ecosystem Investment Partners (EIP) is a real assets, private equity firm with over $1B in AUM and a national footprint that develops institutional capital solutions for the ecological restoration markets. 

EIP delivers high-quality restoration and conservation projects across the United States by acquiring, restoring, and permanently protecting priority conservation properties, and selling the mitigation credits to customers who must offset their unavoidable environmental impacts.  

EIP is one of the country's leading providers of environmental restoration with 80 active mitigation banks and large-scale restoration projects across 16 states. To date, EIP has restored and conserved over 59,000 acres of wetlands, 260 miles of streams, and planted 6.2 million trees as part of our ongoing restoration efforts.* 

Mitigation credits from EIP enable public and private customers to satisfy the requirements of environmental permits quickly and cost-effectively while eliminating liability exposure.  EIP's mitigation banks are approved by the US Army Corps of Engineers and other agencies to fulfill mitigation requirements of Section 404 Clean Water Act and Endangered Species Act permits, when impacts to wetlands, streams, and endangered species are unavoidable.   

*Data as of 12/31/24.

Learn more at www.ecosystempartners.com.

EIP delivers high-quality restoration and conservation projects across the United States by acquiring, restoring, and permanently protecting priority conservation properties, and selling the mitigation credits to customers who must offset their unavoidable environmental impacts.  

EIP is one of the country's leading providers of environmental restoration with 80 active mitigation banks and large-scale restoration projects across 16 states. To date, EIP has restored and conserved over 59,000 acres of wetlands, 260 miles of streams, and planted 6.2 million trees as part of our ongoing restoration efforts.* 

Mitigation credits from EIP enable public and private customers to satisfy the requirements of environmental permits quickly and cost-effectively while eliminating liability exposure.  EIP's mitigation banks are approved by the US Army Corps of Engineers and other agencies to fulfill mitigation requirements of Section 404 Clean Water Act and Endangered Species Act permits, when impacts to wetlands, streams, and endangered species are unavoidable.   

*Data as of 12/31/24.

Learn more at www.ecosystempartners.com.

Title: Co-Founder
Company Product and Services: management consulting , strategy development, business transformation, change management, sales and marketing effectiveness, gtm strategy, growth strategy, value creation , organizational effectiveness, operating model design, governance and decision rights, budgeting, capability development , leadership development , integration support
Company Description:
Oak Cliff Consulting helps lower and middle market executives execute with confidence. 

We founded Oak Cliff Consulting because we believe there is a gap in how lower and middle market companies are served. As former executives and management consultants, we have seen firsthand that these companies are often left with a poor set of choices: overpay for large, inflexible consulting teams, rely on fragmented advisory support, or attempt to navigate increasing complexity alone. 

We believe most executives and leadership teams have a vision, strategy, and an established operating model. Yet there is still a gap between expectations and execution. Results stall not for lack of direction, but for lack of consistent execution. 

We call these breakdowns execution 'fault lines', and they are especially common in the lower and middle market, where complexity is rising:

- Misaligned expectations 
- Capability gaps 
- Ineffective teaming
- Cultural stagnation 

We have developed a proprietary execution system specifically designed for the middle market. Our unique approach is right-sized, flexible, and tailored to the unique needs of each organization.  

Our execution system is grounded in practice and combines strategy consulting rigor with real-world operating experience. We partner with CEOs and executive teams through three types of engagements: 

(1) Align: Single-day executive sessions focused on the most critical areas of need 
(2) Activate: Targeted coaching, development, and accountability to ensure progress sticks
(3) Advise: Custom advisory solutions to support your most important priorities  

If you are leading a company in the lower or middle market and sense a growing gap between intentions and execution, you are not alone. We are here to help.

Title: Managing Partner

Company Description:
It’s always harder than you think. That’s why who you build with matters.

We are not theorists or financiers from afar. We are operators first, proven in the field, not just on paper. We've been in the trenches, sleeves rolled, heart pounding, payroll due. We know the loneliness of search, the weight of the first 100 days, and what it feels like when the deal falls through and there's no one to call.

We back searchers and operators with hunger, grit, and integrity. People building things that matter to real customers, patients, and communities. We invest our own money behind exceptional talent, and we invest ourselves: our time, our scars, our network, our presence.

If you're determined to see it through, we're right there with you.

–

TBD is an investment partnership helmed by Dmitri Ivanov. After Wharton and years in consulting and acquisitions, Dmitri became a searcher, acquired Metro Vein Centers, and spent eight years turning it into the largest search-backed healthcare company in the country. He and his operating partner Andrew Provost built it primarily through de novo expansion, location by location, from scratch. Their outcome is widely recognized as the most successful search fund outcome in the past 20 years. They didn't inherit a playbook. They wrote a new one.

Through TBD, Dmitri is doing what the best operators eventually do: multiply themselves - backing searchers and operators with determination and championing them while they build.

Company Description:
Signal & Engine is a San Jose-based acquisition and operating company. We are local Bay Area operators looking to buy and run one established small business for the long term — built on service, reputation, repeat customers, and strong teams.

We are not private equity. We are not looking to flip a company. Our backgrounds in operations, data, marketing, and AI help us strengthen the systems behind a business — scheduling, reporting, admin — without changing what customers already trust.

What we look for.
Founder-led businesses in the greater San Jose and SF Bay Area; $2M–$15M in revenue; $750K–$2M in SDE/EBITDA; durable local demand; repeat, recurring, contract, or relationship-driven revenue; transferable owner roles; and ten or more employees. Priority verticals: specialty B2B and facility services, specialty distribution and wholesale, and light manufacturing and job shops.

What we believe.
The people and the relationships are the business. A good business doesn't need to be reinvented. Modernization should be invisible to customers. Ownership is a long-term commitment.

For brokers, sellers, and intermediaries: visit signalandengine.com or reach Bob Briski directly via LinkedIn. We respond to every conversation with discretion and respect.

Company Description:
Amerivest Advisors is a Merger & Acquisition firm offering exceptional transactional and financial expertise to lower middle market companies seeking a divestiture, merger, acquisition, recapitalization, or growth capital through a variety of time tested mechanisms.

With a team of accomplished deal makers, Amerivest offers a unique blend of sophisticated solutions and extraordinary client service to an underserved segment of the market. The firm is comprised of seasoned professionals who have diverse backgrounds in Mergers & Acquisitions, strategic planning, corporate development and corporate finance. Our team has worked extensively as principals and intermediaries in structuring and closing transactions with an aggregate value in excess of $250 million.

At Amerivest, we create attractive alternatives based on the individual needs and objectives of our clients and assist them in realizing the maximum achievable value of their businesses. Our goal is simply to preserve and enhance the value of wealth our clients have created.

Amerivest is singularly focused on the lower middle market and has developed extensive relationships with potential suitors who have specifically targeted this segment.

Everyone tends to define the lower middle market differently. Here is how we see it. Companies in this space tend to have the following attributes:
Lower middle market companies are typically Privately-Held
Lower middle market companies are typically generating EBITDA between $2 million to $10 million
Lower middle market Companies are typically generating between $10 and $100 million in revenues.

Company Description: Founded in 2009, Corre Partners is an alternative credit manager focused on transitional and turnaround credits in the U.S. middle market, a large and target-rich segment of the corporate credit universe.  Corre’s strategy seeks to capitalize on the growing structural change in the middle market that drives pricing dislocations and inefficiencies for borrowers facing transitory issues.  By tactically investing across middle market capital structures, Corre seeks to generate equity-like returns while assuming less risk by creating a differentiated portfolio that blends low risk credit, event driven and stressed credit, and select equities via restructurings or special situations.  The firm specifically targets under-followed middle market situations undergoing temporal stress and employs a rigorous private-equity style underwriting process along with uniquely active engagement to help accelerate and maximize positive outcomes in this evergreen opportunity set independent of macro cycles.

Company Description:
Unbridled Automation Group is a boutique consulting firm built for medium to large industrial enterprises pursuing automation transformation and operational excellence.

We are operators first. Our principals bring decades of hands-on executive experience across some of North America's most demanding manufacturing environments — spanning robotics, digital twin, MES, IoT, connected factory systems, lean material flow, AMR deployment, and inbound supply chain redesign. We have built automation organizations from scratch, restructured global supply chains, validated nine-figure capital investments, and been accountable for results at enterprise scale.

We do not sell technology. We do not implement systems. We provide independent strategic and organizational expertise that sits above the technology layer — the kind that only comes from having run it.

Core Service Areas
 - Automation Strategy & Systems Development
 - Robotics & Advanced Automation Advisory
 - Digital Twin, MES & Connected Factory Systems
 - Material Flow & Inbound Logistics Transformation
 - Capital Project Advisory

Who We Serve
Large manufacturers and industrial enterprises that need independent, senior-level expertise to execute transformation confidently — whether building new capability, validating a critical investment, or providing executive surge capacity on a high-stakes initiative.

The market has no shortage of consultants who have studied manufacturing. It has a shortage of consultants who have run it. Every engagement is principal-led. No junior staff. No methodology decks substituting for experience.

Title: Strategy Partnerships Director
Company Product and Services: cost segregation, tax credits, r&d tax credits, business financial optimization, capital recovery, financial strategy, business advisory, strategic partnerships, referral partner programs
Company Description:
Biz Savvy Network helps business owners identify and unlock hidden financial opportunities within their companies.

Many businesses focus only on revenue and expenses, but significant capital opportunities often exist inside the tax code, operational structure, and financial strategy of the company.

Our team specializes in helping businesses discover and access:

• Federal and state tax credits
• Cost segregation strategies for real estate owners
• R&D tax incentives
• Payroll and employment-related credits
• Strategic financial optimization opportunities

Through our Biz Savvy Financial Opportunity Map, we analyze a company's financial structure to identify areas where additional capital may be recovered, reinvested, or optimized.

We work with business owners, CPAs, financial professionals, real estate investors, and strategic partners to help companies improve their financial position and accelerate growth.

Our goal is simple:

Help businesses keep more of the money they earn and reinvest it into growth.

Services Include

• Financial Opportunity Reviews
• Tax Credit Identification
• Cost Segregation Analysis
• Capital Optimization Strategies
• Strategic Partner Programs

If you are a business owner, CPA, consultant, or referral partner interested in identifying financial opportunities for businesses, we invite you to connect with us.

Company Description:
Founded in 2015, Atticus Franchise Group is an Atlanta-based private equity firm focused on consumer and retail companies, with a specialization in franchised and multi-unit business models primarily in the Southeast (in addition to the Southeast, we currently have locations in the Mountain Region and Mid-West). Atticus focuses on franchise concepts with a compelling value proposition, meaningful barriers-to-entry, predictable cash flows, and identifiable growth. Through our industry relationships, advisory board, lending partners, and previous experience of franchise operations, our team looks to create a competitive advantage for outsized returns to our investors.  Currently, Atticus owns and operates Massage Envy, Wingstop, and Maaco Franchise locations.

Atticus Franchise Group was named after Atticus Finch, in the novel “To Kill a Mockingbird”. Atticus, who was a lawyer, was respected and admired by his peers for being fair, flexible, and steadfastly honest. When faced with tough decisions, he exemplified penetrating intelligence, calm wisdom, exemplary behavior, and absolute consistency. Atticus represents morality, reason, positivity, and brings out the best in other people. Atticus Franchise Group’s investment style, core values, and business philosophy are meant to exemplify the traits and qualities that Atticus Finch embodied throughout the novel.

Company Description: Fortis is a team of experienced M&A professionals who serve as the post-closing representative of selling shareholders in private company mergers and acquisitions. Shareholders are increasingly recognizing that it is a task they should not undertake and are looking for an alternative.  Fortis is the solution.

Title: Founder
Company Description:
Private equity firms have more visibility into 
portfolio company financials than at any point 
in the industry's history. Dashboards, real-time 
reporting, KPI tracking — the downstream data 
layer is well-developed.

The upstream layer isn't.

The intelligence that actually determines whether 
a hold period succeeds — how executives reason 
under pressure, where leadership teams are 
misaligned, whether a turnaround plan is 
executing or deflecting — lives in conversation. 
It always has. It has just never had an 
analytical layer.

Edgemont is a voice-first conversational AI 
platform built for private equity. The platform 
conducts structured conversations with portfolio 
company executives on a defined cadence, 
synthesizes behavioral signal across individuals 
and time, and surfaces the patterns that don't 
appear in board decks: decision orientation, 
alignment gaps, execution drift, and early 
indicators of risk.

The output is longitudinal and evidence-grounded. 
Every signal is supported by the conversational 
evidence that produced it. Every trend is 
traceable to the specific behavioral shifts that 
drove it.

Edgemont runs a limited number of engagements 
per quarter. The work is specific to the hold 
period — where the gap between reported progress 
and operational reality is widest, and where 
early intelligence has the most value.

Title: Founder & CEO
Company Description:
The tools that help institutions allocate capital have never been accessible to everyone else. A Bloomberg Terminal costs $25,000 a year. Goldman Sachs charges $500,000 for an advisory mandate. The individual investor — regardless of how much they know or how hard they work — gets Yahoo Finance and a broker who earns commission when they trade. That asymmetry is not an accident. It is a design.

VInvest breaks the design.

We are building the operating system for fundamental investing: a hallucination-free valuation engine that covers 6,000+ US companies, runs six models simultaneously on every ticker, and delivers a single number — the Goldline — that tells any investor whether the current price is justified. Updated dynamically as earnings drop, filings land, and rates move. Free to check, on any US ticker, before you trade anywhere.

The platform is live.

Every company is scored on four pillars — Quality, Value, Safety, and Momentum — to produce a V-Rating from 0 to 100. Every output carries a Confidence Score derived from 12 auditable checkpoints. Every assumption is transparent and traceable to a source. Sophisticated investors can enter the Valuation Lab, adjust the AI-extracted driver assumptions, blend their own model weights, and save their version of intrinsic value to a Circle of Competence of 20 tickers. The Rationalist AI generates a full investment memorandum — executive summary, risk factors, valuation deep-dive, and a grounded Buy / Hold / Avoid recommendation — on any Pro ticker, on demand.

This is institutional-grade analysis. At $29 a month.

Coming next: Social Exchange, where every investment claim is anchored to a model. Intelligent Broker execution via Logic-Snap. Global markets — UK, Canada, Europe, India, Asia Pacific — within six months.

The mission is simple: capital allocated by rigorous analysis, not by information privilege. Wealth built on fundamental truth, accessible to every investor in every market.

vinvest.ai

Company Description:
RMS is engaged by its clients to make referrals to buyers or sellers of securities. If such client closes a securities transaction with a buyer or seller so referred, RMS is entitled to a success fee from the client. Such success fee may be in the form of cash or in warrants to purchase securities of the client or client’s affiliate. It is not uncommon for RMS or RMS representatives to hold equity in its issuer clients or in the issuers of securities purchased or sold by the parties to a transaction. 

This communication does not represent an offer or solicitation to buy or sell securities. Such an offer must be made via definitive legal documentation by the buyer or seller of securities. RMS deals only in securities offered in private placements (“Securities”). The Securities are not approved or disapproved by the SEC or any other federal or state agency. No regulatory agency has endorsed the accuracy or adequacy of this communication or any offer or solicitation to buy or sell the Securities. 

Only investors that qualify as “accredited investors” - as defined Rule 501(d) of the Securities Act of 1933 - may invest in the Securities. Investments in the Securities are speculative and involve a high degree of risk. An investor in the Securities should have little to no need for liquidity in the foreseeable future and should be able to withstand the loss of the entire investment. Potential buyers or sellers of the Securities should seek professional counsel prior to entering into any transaction.

Title: Principal
Company Description:
Auxo Investment Partners makes investments in companies at transition points in their history, then instills the infrastructure, processes and talent that nurture their success and help them grow to their full potential. We do so in collaboration with our management teams and investors – as our name reflects, we are not merely investors, but partners.

We are veteran investor-operators,  having a long and successful track record of providing strategic operational guidance to help companies unlock growth and achieve scale without sacrificing their legacies. 
 
Our investment philosophy also reflects our belief in a broader set of obligations: 

> Long-Term Investors: We desire to own companies for extended periods of time. Our unique structure and investment model aligns investors with the company’s interests. This allows us to avoid the excessive leverage and short-term decision making that beset many private equity firms.

> Trusted Stewards: We care deeply about the families, employees, communities and customers of the companies we invest in. We take seriously the responsibility and trust placed in us as stewards of the next chapter of a company’s history.

> A Human Endeavor: We believe business is a human endeavor, not a spreadsheet exercise. We invest in people and partner with management teams in guiding companies to reach their fullest potential.

> Supporting U.S. Manufacturing: We believe domestic manufacturing makes America and American families stronger, so we strive to preserve and create US manufacturing jobs.

> Conscious Capitalism: We adhere to the values enumerated in the United Nations Principles of Responsible Investing and integrate environmental, social and corporate governance (ESG) concerns into our investment practices.

For more information, please visit the firm’s website at www.auxopartners.com.

Title: Director and Head of AI
Company Product and Services: first lien loans and revolvers, unitranche loans, second lien loans, mezzanine debt, debtor-in-possession facilities, clos
Company Description:
MC Credit Partners LP ("MCCP") offers creative and comprehensive financing solutions to middle market companies. Part of MCCP Investment Partners LP, MCCP is an alternative investment manager offering clients complementary solutions across middle market direct lending, structured equity and collateralized loan obligations.

MCCP capitalizes on what it believes to be a significant opportunity in middle market lending. MCCP is led by Ashok Nayyar who has over 30 years of experience in corporate debt and loans having served as the Head of North American Leveraged Finance at Citigroup, the Head of Global Leveraged Finance at Morgan Stanley, and the Founder and former Chief Investment Officer of Cyan Partners LP. The senior MCCP investment team possess extensive lending experience as well as complementary skills in M&A, private equity, mezzanine lending, turnarounds, workouts, legal, and accounting. Together, MCCP’s senior investment team possess more than 350 years of collective sourcing, underwriting and investment management experience in corporate debt and loans, and have provided more than $500 billion of capital for companies across a variety of industries.

Company Description:
altrntv labs is an independent analysis practice for tokenized financial products. We work with issuers preparing products for capital and allocators evaluating whether to deploy into them.

The products we assess include tokenized funds, private credit strategies, yield-bearing instruments, and other real-world financial assets brought onchain. Tokenization changes how financial products are structured, distributed, and accessed. It does not automatically make them underwritable, liquid, or ready for allocation.

We evaluate products across structure, economics, operations, distribution, and liquidity. The work surfaces what a product depends on, where it is exposed, and what needs to change before it can reach the right capital.

Our work includes issuer assessments, allocator product assessments, focused tokenization advisory, and sector-level research.

Company Description:
Fulcrum & Co. is a diagnostic-driven leadership advisory firm. We measure what no one else measures — the structural integrity of the leadership system underneath every company.

Founded in 2013, we work with CEOs, founders, and leadership teams at companies between $3M and $800M in revenue. Our proprietary instrument suite — the Leadership Equilibrium Method™ — assesses four interdependent pillars: Business Mastery, Personal Evolution, Physical Vitality, and Purpose & Legacy. The resulting diagnostic (EQI™) quantifies where a company’s growth is compounding and where it’s quietly eroding.

Most advisory firms prescribe before they diagnose. We reverse that order. Every engagement begins with a comprehensive leadership system assessment. The findings inform everything that follows — from fractional executive work to board-level advisory to full transformation programs.

Our founder, Ryan Erickson, has built and sold two companies ($100M+ exit and $50M ARR SaaS), served as COO of a 250-person organization scaled from $9M to $20M, and mentored 20+ CEOs through inflection points ranging from $5M to $800M. Fulcrum & Co. is the formal brand of a practice he’s been running since 2013.

We measure what no one else measures. Then we fix what the measurement reveals.

Scale-Up Planning — I help facilities move from pilot to production, or from one facility to multiple, anticipating the traps and opportunities that manifest at scale.
Vendor-Side Advisory — I work alongside provider teams to help their customers bridge the gap between equipment capability and operational reality.

ExecHQ helps companies drive growth and profitability by providing high-level, on-demand executive expertise through its "Executives as a Service" (ExaaS) model. We connect institutional capital with the assets, energy, and intelligence shaping the future of digital infrastructure. By translating market transformation into disciplined opportunity, we help investors move ahead of demand driven by AI, cloud, and hyperscale growth. 

Every investment follows one core principle: capital should lead innovation. Through a global network of developers, operators, and partners, we build portfolios designed for adaptability, resilience, and long-term performance.

Title: Managing Partner
Company Product and Services: consumer, manufacturing, industrial, investment banking, private equity, mergers & acquisitions, family businesses, founder led
Company Description:
Carter Morse & Goodrich is a boutique M&A advisory firm that exclusively represents founder-led and family-held companies typically valued between $25 million and $250 million. 

CMG advises owners pursuing their once-in-a-lifetime sale of their business as they plan, prepare, and close successful transactions. We fully understand and appreciate the unique dynamics of these companies, the objective of maximizing value and the importance of owner legacies. 
 
We work with leading companies in unique and niche markets leveraging our industry expertise in manufacturing, consumer products, food and beverage, business services and HVAC. 
 
Since 1987, the combination of our hands-on approach, senior banker attention, strategic guidance, seamless transaction execution and extensive network of domestic and international resources has enabled us to become a trusted advisor to hundreds of business owners.

Caste Capital is a Boston-based special situations real estate private equity firm. Since our founding in 2021, we have opportunistically invested across complementary asset classes and distinctive opportunities that significantly influence the built environment.

Title: Director
Company Description: Long Wharf Capital LLC is a Boston-based private equity real estate manager focused exclusively on value-added investments in the U.S.  Formed in 2011, the firm invests on behalf of institutional clients including pension funds, endowments, foundations, and family offices. Title: Senior Director
Company Product and Services: mergers & acquisitions, strategic growth, corporate finance, trusted owner advisory, exit planning, esops, value enhancement, capital sourcing, ownership transition planning
Company Description:
Footprint Capital is a boutique investment bank focused on advising owners and operators of privately held companies in the middle market. We help business owners write the next chapter of their story, whether that is leaving a legacy, turning a corner, or forging down a new path. Securities offered through KCOE Capital LLC, Member FINRA.

Deeply rooted in the Midwest, Footprint Capital is proudly based in Columbus, Ohio. Our services include sell-side and buy-side mergers and acquisitions, assisting with internal transactions such as management buyouts and ESOPs, ownership transition planning, growth consulting, value enhancement, capital sourcing, and strategic management consulting.

Each situation is unique. Many middle market owners, CEO’s, and entrepreneurs could use some help determining which path to choose. We are here to help, to earn trust, and to deliver successful outcomes.

Title: Founder | CFO Step
Company Product and Services: bookkeeping, fractional cfo, cash flow forecasting, fp&a, m&a advisory, capital raises, financial strategy, controller services, kpi reporting, financial planning & analysis, budget development, financial modeling, exit preparation, cash management, accounting systems implementation, due diligence, post-acquisition integration, pricing strategy, profitability analysis
Company Description:
Financial Clarity at Every Stage of Growth.

Every business hits a point where the finances get more complex than a spreadsheet can handle. Maybe you're growing fast and cash flow is getting harder to predict. Maybe you're making bigger decisions and need more than a basic P&L to guide them. Maybe you just need someone to keep the books clean so you can focus on running your business.
That's why CFO Step exists.

We provide bookkeeping, cash flow forecasting, KPI reporting, and fractional CFO services for growing businesses. Our team brings over 20 years of hands-on CFO and senior finance experience across multi-unit restaurants and hospitality, e-commerce, healthcare, manufacturing, construction, and professional services. We've scaled businesses from $350 million to over $1.4 billion in revenue, led M&A on multi-billion-dollar transactions, and built finance functions from the ground up in PE-backed, high-growth environments.

Our approach is simple. Start with a clean financial foundation. Build consistency. Add visibility. Then step into strategic financial leadership when you're ready. No long-term contracts, no bloated retainers — just the right level of support for where you are right now.

Whether you need reliable monthly bookkeeping or a financial partner at the table for the decisions that matter most, we meet you where you are and help you take the next step.

cfostep.com

Title: Founder and CEO
Company Product and Services: keynote speaker, business consulting, fractional leadership, executive coaching, hospitality industry, growth companies, analysis, restaurants, non-profit, start-ups, private equity, team building, strategic planning, employee experience, guest experience, retention
Company Description:
Complexity kills. Simplicity scales.

Leadership Simplicity helps organizations cut through unnecessary complexity, narrow their focus, and dramatically magnify their impact.

We believe effective leadership and high-performing organizations don’t need to be complicated. The best results come from clarity, intentionality, and disciplined simplicity. Our mission is to help leaders and teams simplify their lives and leadership so they can move faster, execute better, and build sustainable success without burnout.

With deep roots in the hospitality industry and extensive experience across service-driven sectors, Leadership Simplicity partners with executives, leadership teams, and growing organizations ready to trade busyness for measurable results. We specialize in helping hotels, restaurants, multi-unit operators, and high-standard service businesses eliminate drag and strengthen what truly drives performance.

Our Services:
- Keynote Speaking: High-energy, practical presentations that challenge leaders to embrace simplicity as a powerful competitive advantage.
- Business Consulting & Analysis: Objective, data-driven evaluations of strategy, operations, and organizational design to uncover leverage points and reduce complexity.
- Executive Coaching: Personalized coaching for leaders seeking greater clarity, confidence, and calm authority.
- Team Building & Training: Engaging workshops and programs that align teams around shared priorities, improve communication, and foster disciplined execution.
- Fractional Leadership: Flexible, fractional leadership support designed specifically for hospitality and service organizations that need senior expertise without full-time overhead.

Our approach is straightforward, battle-tested, and rooted in real-world hospitality leadership. We help organizations simplify what matters most: focus, people, processes, and results.

If your organization is ready to simplify what’s complicated and scale what truly matters, let's connect!

Company Description:
Northcoast is currently looking to expand our sales team. Senior and Associate level positions available.

Northcoast Research is an independent institutional equity research firm located in Cleveland, OH. Founded in 2009, our professional staff includes  Senior Research Analysts and  Associate Research Analysts, who publish, and distribute, industry and individual public company research to institutional investors nationally. Coverage is focused on a growing list of  companies within four industry verticals: Business Services, Consumer, Basic Materials & Industrials as well as Healthcare. The Research team additionally sponsors various types of corporate access events, including corporate management non-deal roadshows, industry field trips, industry expert conference calls, etc.

Northcoast Research’s veteran sales and trading  team adds value by combining extensive knowledge of the companies and Industries covered with  a keen sense for clients'​ specific investment styles and objectives and provides our clients superior trade execution, employing Exchange floor brokers, ECN’s, algorithms, and FIX protocols.

Title: Founder/Owner
Company Description:
The Fractional Network is a curated community of high-level fractional executives and executive recruiters built on one core belief: collaboration over competition. This is not a transactional networking group or a room full of sales pitches. It is an operator-first environment designed for experienced leaders to build meaningful relationships, exchange real-world insight, and create opportunities together.

The network brings together Fractional CEOs, COOs, CFOs, CMOs, CTOs, CHROs, CROs, and other senior operators from across the country who are actively helping businesses scale, optimize operations, improve profitability, and navigate growth. Executive recruiters are also integrated into the community to build long-term relationships with proven leadership talent before opportunities arise.

The Fractional Network exists to elevate the future of fractional leadership by creating a trusted environment where experienced operators can collaborate, challenge one another, and share practical insight from real business experience. Members are not theorists. They are practitioners who have led organizations through growth, transformation, operational complexity, and strategic change.

The community meets bi-weekly in a mastermind-style format focused on leadership, operations, growth strategy, AI, organizational design, private equity, sales, culture, and scaling systems. Meetings are intentionally structured to encourage participation, strategic thinking, and relationship-building through collaborative discussion and curated breakout groups.

The network has already generated meaningful business opportunities, executive placements, partnerships, referrals, and speaking engagements for its members. As it continues to grow, The Fractional Network is expanding into a larger ecosystem that includes executive resources, leadership events, recruiter partnerships, and strategic business collaboration.

The mission is simple: connect exceptional leaders in a trusted environment

Title: Director of Client Relations at Bay Point Advisors LLC
Company Product and Services: top performing income fund, private debt, low volatility returns
Company Description:
Bay Point Advisors, LLC is an investment advisory firm focused on absolute value, high current income and low volatility returns.  We emphasize performance over growth and have a contrarian bias to investing.  

The firm originates and invests in short-term, senior secured, deep loan-to-value bridge loans to small businesses, specialty finance and seasoned real estate holdings in the US.  We allocate capital to niche markets that are difficult to access, require in-depth research, and are less efficient. We seek to avoid the disruptive forces associated when massive capital flows overwhelm income generating sectors in the relentless search for yield.  

Since the banking crisis of 2008/2009, increased regulatory pressure on banks has diminished the flow of capital to small businesses, and significantly increased the need for private lending.  While many of these remote markets have existed for decades, and have a history of generating attractive returns, the disruption of the sovereign bond markets by central banks and the loss of confidence in the credit markets after the financial crisis has increased the relative isolation of these niche markets.  Low central bank policy rates, bank consolidation and increased regulation have resulted in massive capital flows into the government debt of developed countries and the corporate debt of large companies through the issuance of corporate bonds or syndicated loans.  These large capital flows to governments and corporations have effectively lowered the cost of capital for good and bad performers alike, while starving most small businesses of growth capital.  Bay Point has stepped in to fill the void in small business lending.

Title: Managing Director, Valuations & Acquisitions
Company Product and Services: mergers and acquisitions, home care, exit planning, valuation, intermediary
Company Description:
Headquartered in Chesterfield, Mo., Osborne HomeCare Group is a national acquisitions intermediary focused on assisting clients in buying, selling, or expanding a home care business. 

Exit Strategies: In a fragmented industry that consists primarily of Managing Owners, Exit Strategy has two meanings. One is exiting from the responsibility of running the business. The second is exiting the investment. Most owners accomplish both when their companies are acquired. Some, however, prefer to “cash in their chips” while retaining an operating role in the company. Others seek to untether from the business while retaining ownership. The right strategy is situational. We’ll help you sort through the alternatives to make a well informed decision about the right one for you.

Expansion Initiatives: Although the home care industry continues to grow organically from expanding demand, some owners seek to accelerate growth inorganically through strategic acquisitions. The endeavor is not without its risks. An Acquisition Target’s past performance is not necessarily indicative of future results. But often it is based on other Key Predictive Indicators such as Managing Owner involvement, caregiver retention, and regulatory or competitive threats. We’ll help you with the Ideal Acquisition Profile on the front end and the prospects for earnings sustainability during the search.  Our deal sourcing is enabled by national outreach capabilities focused primarily on “off-market” home care providers and secondarily by monitoring on-market activity.

Entry Investments: The 2.8 Trillion dollar US healthcare system now recognizes the importance of home care and the positive outcomes it creates. As such, the home care industry continues to benefit from increased visibility. It represents an attractive opportunity among financial investors and investor-operators seeking to acquire an existing home care platform. In today’s home care landscape, start-ups are becoming rare as it’s difficult for them to compete with providers that have established reputations, office teams, referral providers, and caregivers.  Whether among our current on-market opportunities or through a strategic search, we’ll help you find the right entry door.

Alpha Capital Family Office is an independent financial advisory firm with a singular mission: to assist clients in managing their family, business, and wealth.  Alpha Capital's inception as an independent entity was driven by a commitment to improving client service and maintaining control over the client experience. They saw many large financial institutions more interested in their own goals and bottom lines and not that of their clients. Alpha Capital is different and clients value that in many ways; they are a team comprised of three generations, fiduciaries, and strategic and independent thinkers for their clients’ needs. 

Alpha Capital's exclusive dedication is to its clients, with an emphasis on:

What is important to them?

- Knowing the family, their values, their goals
- Helping the family Succeed on Purpose 
- Providing Generational planning and development

The firm addresses the financial needs of families, businesses, and wealth through:

- Cash flow now and in the future
- Tax mitigation
- Taking care of family and heirs
- Protecting their wealth from unforeseen liabilities and litigation
- Maximizing family and charitable giving

Alpha Capital provides clients with valuable tools, including the Wealth Planning Timeline, Tax Planning Worksheet, and Executive Summary. Their approach introduces a level of organization and proactive planning giving clients more time to enjoy other priorities in life. Alpha Capital extends its services nationally, with its headquarters located in Denver, Colorado.

Title: Managing Director
Company Product and Services: commercial real estate, investment sales, project finance, corporate finance, real estate investment banking
Company Description:
Greenwich Group International is a real estate investment banking firm with market-leading expertise in all aspects of real estate investment capital, focused on maximizing owner value in complex real estate situations. Since its inception in 1995, Greenwich has become one of the most active real estate investment banking and brokerage firms in the United States. We have successfully undertaken more than $45 billion in real estate sales, equity and debt financings, and capital raising activities.

Greenwich operates from offices in four strategic US gateway cities: New York, Los Angeles, San Francisco & Washington, DC, and maintains strong ties to the capital markets of Asia, the Middle East and Europe through its affiliated companies.

Greenwich is a real estate investment banking and capital advisory firm with deep experience in capital markets and unsurpassed access to international and domestic flows of capital. 
Our expertise extends across five business lines:

Capital Markets (Debt & Equity Placement) 
Investment Sales
Investment Banking and Structured Finance 
Development Services
Middle Markets

Greenwich has successfully undertaken more than $45 billion in real estate sales, equity and debt financings, and fundraising activities – making us one of the most active real estate investment advisory and brokerage firms in the world. Our staff has an average tenure of 15 years, delivering invaluable continuity in longstanding relationships and structuring knowledge.

Greenwich is uniquely qualified to source optimal, competitive capital for owners and developers in the form of equity and debt or disposition of real estate assets. Thanks to our continuous involvement in the investment market, we have developed an expert understanding of the industry as a whole. Our team is in constant contact with investors around the globe to discuss investment opportunities, access market conditions, and monitor industry trends in investment criteria and appetite

Title: Senior Managing Partner
Company Description:
You spent decades building your business. Don't hand it to someone who'll gut it.
Private equity wants a quick flip. Individual buyers can't get financing. Family isn't interested.
There's a third option: Sell to your employees.
At Forhemit, we help owners transfer ownership to the people who actually built the company. You get paid—often more than a traditional sale, with significant tax advantages. Your team gets a stake in what they helped create. The business stays independent and in expert hands.
We handle the entire process: financing, legal coordination, and execution—typically closing in 90-120 days.
We work with:
→ Business owners exploring exit options
→ Brokers who need a serious, funded buyer
→ Lenders who want clean, closable deals
No jargon. No surprises. Just a clear path to an exit that works for everyone.

Company Description:
The Food Partners, headquartered in Washington DC, is the nation’s preeminent investment banking firm to the food industry. TFP provides merger, acquisition and divestiture services, private placements of debt and equity capital, financial restructuring, strategic advisory and loan portfolio services. Clients represent all segments of the food chain and include input, processing, manufacturing, distribution and retail companies as well as the most prominent capital sources to the food industry. TFP serves clients from the Washington DC office and across the nation.

By utilizing TFP’s services, clients rely on a team of professionals that has:
 - Advised on more than 75 mergers, acquisitions and divestitures, representing total consideration in excess of $5.0 billion.
 - Advised in the placement of more than $10.0 billion of financing for clients.
 - Advised more privately held food wholesalers and retailers on mergers, acquisitions and divestitures than any other group in the industry.
 - Advised nearly every major agricultural and food distribution cooperative in the country.
 - Created the food industry’s most comprehensive databases for private and public food industry transactions, potential capital sources, and middle market food companies.
 - Established and obtained funding for more than 25 ESOPs.

Operational stabilization for organizations in transition

Title: Founder & CEO
Company Description:
CreatorArq is the M&A advisory firm built for the creator economy. We work with mega creators on YouTube, Instagram, and LinkedIn whose businesses have outgrown the talent model and are ready to be acquired. Most creators are stuck on the hamster wheel: high revenue, no enterprise value, nothing a PE firm or strategic buyer can underwrite. We architect the path off the wheel.

Our work covers the full exit arc: cleaning up IP and trademark ownership, restructuring the operating company, building the recurring revenue and data assets that drive multiples, and running the deal process to acquisition. We pair domain authority (built over an 18-year Hollywood career and three years representing the top tier of creators) with institutional capital relationships and an operational backbone purpose-built for this market. The result is a creator business that sells, at multiples that look like enterprise software rather than influencer marketing.

If you’re a creator doing 7-8 figures and wondering what the exit actually looks like, that’s the conversation we have.

Title: Founder & Partner
Company Description:
At World Bridge Capital, we are building a more inclusive financial ecosystem one where businesses of all sizes, regardless of location, have access to the liquidity and opportunities needed to grow within global supply chains.

While large corporations rely on debt and established credit lines to manage cash flow, many small and lower middle market (LMM) companies are left without the same financial tools. This gap limits their ability to scale, compete, and operate efficiently in an increasingly interconnected world.

World Bridge Capital exists to change that.

We provide alternative financing solutions designed to unlock working capital, improve cash flow, and empower businesses to participate fully in global trade. Our approach is built on accessibility, flexibility, and a deep understanding of the challenges faced by underserved markets.

If your business is looking for new ways to access liquidity and accelerate growth, we’re here to help.

Title: Founder
Company Product and Services: sales methodology, sales coaching, sales enablement, sales leadership consulting, executive consulting, mortgage industry consulting, financial services advisory, sales training consulting, executive coaching, revenue growth strategy, recruiting and retention, partnership development, team development, referral strategy, branch and producer development, fintech institutional access advisory,
Company Description:
Scott Kuhn Advisory is the home of four proprietary frameworks that build sharper individual contributors, more focused sales leaders, and more productive sales organizations.

The HUC Method is a conversation framework that turns every client interaction into a closed transaction. Heard, Understood, Clear, in that order, on every conversation. Built inside finance, adapted to any consultative sales role, organization, or institution.

The Peer Principal is the doctrine that you walk into every sales conversation as a principal, not an agent. It is how individual contributors and sales leaders earn the trust that closes the transaction in front of them, and builds the referral network that compounds for decades.

Marble in the Jar is the relationship-recovery doctrine that names the silent grievance every fragile client relationship carries, and the move that pulls it out.

The Shared Custody Model is the institutional governance framework that reframes the most revealing trust event a client has with a sales organization, and shows boards and senior leaders how to govern that trust for enterprise value rather than departmental gain. Published as the book Shared Custody: Why Mortgage Trust Determines Enterprise Value.

The advisory works through one-on-one engagements, team licensing, on-site workshops, and board and executive consultations. Every engagement is designed to install the frameworks into daily practice and produce measurable change inside the first 30 to 90 days.

Founded by Scott Kuhn, a 30-year sales executive.

Company Description:
If your business is growing but your operations can't keep up, it's not a people problem. It's a structural problem.

ARCH Advisory & Consulting Services designs the internal infrastructure that allows organizations to scale with discipline: operating models, governance frameworks, compensation strategy, and people systems.

We partner with founders and operators who have hit the ceiling of how far hustle and improvisation can take them. If decisions are made inconsistently, accountability is unclear, or growth keeps exposing the same gaps, that is the work we do.

Everything we build is grounded in four principles:

Alignment — We design the internal architecture that connects your strategy to your operations, your people to your processes, and your decisions to your outcomes.

Rigor — Every system we design is built with discipline and precision, tested against how your business actually runs, not how it looks on paper.

Clarity — No more ambiguity about roles, decisions, or direction. You leave with a structure you can see, explain, and build on.

Hold — Built to withstand growth, leadership transitions, and operational pressure. These are systems that don't break when things get hard.

Founded by a senior organizational strategist with 20+ years of cross-sector experience, ARCH operates at the intersection of strategy and systems design.

Your business knowledge. Our infrastructure expertise. Systems that hold.

If any of this sounds familiar, let's talk. Feel free to message us here on LinkedIn.

Senior Attention:

Boutique Feel / Global Reach:
A boutique firm is able to create close-knit client relationships, but many boutiques are strictly regional. Our track record includes clients and buyers that span the globe. We provide high levels of client service and a proven ability to reach buyers and sellers around the world.

Company Description:
Molt & Bloom | HR Advisory for Industrial Companies

We are an HR consulting firm helping manufacturers, distribution operations, and supply chain businesses solve complex workforce challenges from labor relations and HR compliance to leadership development and private equity HR integration. If your workforce challenges live on the plant floor, you're in the right place.

Our advisors have worked inside the plants and operations we now advise. Every engagement is scoped before it starts.  We skip the theory and deliver practical solutions.  You know what you're getting, what it costs, and what execution looks like.

Practice Areas:
- Labor Relations — union vulnerability, collective bargaining, grievance management
- Employee Relations — investigations, conflict resolution, Employee Relations strategy
- HR Compliance & Risk — FLSA, FMLA, OSHA, ADA, EEO
- Workforce & Organizational Effectiveness — workforce planning, employee turnover analysis, retention strategy
- Leadership & Talent Development — leadership training, coaching
- Private Equity HR Services — HR due diligence, post-acquisition integration, fractional HR

Who We Serve:
Mid-market manufacturers, distribution and logistics operations, and Private Equity portfolio companies that need straight answers and practical solutions without the overhead of a large firm or a full time HR position.

The name comes from Darwin's finches. When Charles Darwin studied the finches of the Galápagos Islands, he discovered that the species that thrived weren't the strongest or the largest. They were the ones that adapted. 

Molt and Bloom exists on that same premise. The organizations that win aren't always the ones with the most resources. They're the ones willing to shed what isn't working and build something stronger. We help industrial companies do exactly that.  

Serving clients nationally 

If you're managing a workforce challenge that needs a direct answer, reach out to us or visit  https://moltandbloom.com

Title: Co-founder and CEO
Company Description:
Your clients keep asking for private deals, but how do you find them, understand the risks, and trust the deal sponsors & operators?

Argus is the diligence layer for private markets. We look beyond asset class labels to show wealth managers the real risks under the hood, so they can identify the right deals for each client and defend the recommendation if it goes sideways.

Our platform has three layers:

𝟎𝟏. 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐝 𝐠𝐚𝐩𝐬. Factor-based gap analysis on every client portfolio. Asset class labels lie. A portfolio of stocks, real estate, and a direct deal can look diversified and still be a single concentrated bet underneath.

𝟎𝟐. 𝐅𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐠𝐚𝐩𝐬. Every deal in our network is scored against the portfolio the advisor already has. A 30% IRR is meaningless if it doubles down on exposures the household is already drowning in. We rank opportunities by what they actually add: risk-adjusted return, factor balance, liquidity flexibility.

𝟎𝟑. 𝐖𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐟𝐢𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞. Structured diligence corpus on every sponsor in our network. The questions IR teams answer for public companies, now answered for private deals. Plus in-person events for advisors to meet sponsors and operators directly.

Asset allocation got commoditized. Alts is the new value layer. Private markets aren't disintermediating advisory. They're the reason advisory still exists.

Argus Panoptes was the all-seeing giant of Greek myth, set to watch what others could not. We named the company after him because the biggest risks in private markets are the ones nobody is actively looking for.

getargus.tech

Title: Managing Director
Company Description:
Most companies don't wake up thinking,

"You know what we need? A whole new revenue strategy."

Usually it's more like…

"Why is our pipeline all over the place?"

"Why do deals take this long to close?"

"Didn't we already fix this six months ago?"

Things look fine on paper. Then you get into it, and something just stops working. Pipeline slows down. Deals don't close. Marketing's doing one thing. Sales is doing another. Everybody's busy. Nobody's sure why it's not clicking.

That's where Cover6 comes in. We're a full-service revenue agency that helps you fix what's not working and strengthen what is. You don't need to overhaul everything. If one part of your revenue engine is off, we step in there. If you need more, we build it out with you.

Sometimes it's one issue. Sometimes it's a few. Sometimes it's… more than a few. Whatever the need, we focus on outcomes you can actually see: more pipeline, stronger close rates, and faster movement from first conversation to closed deal.

We can handle one piece or we can help run the whole thing.

Either way, we've got your 6.

Revenue AI & Automation
We audit and clean up your tech stack, modernize processes, optimize CRMs, and integrate AI into workflows so your team moves faster with better visibility.

Sales Execution & Leadership
Fractional reps. Fractional leadership. Inside sales. Outbound execution. Pipeline management. Deal acceleration. We plug in where needed and help you build a team that performs without unnecessary overhead.

Revenue Strategy & Advisory
We design sales playbooks, refine go-to-market strategy, tighten positioning and messaging, define ICPs, and build roadmaps that align systems, people, and process.

Integrated Marketing Services
From paid media and social to PR, branding, and email nurture, everything we do supports pipeline and close rates.

Company Description:
CMG Companies is a collection of separate and independent entities, holding controlling investments predominantly in the multi-unit and franchise sector, spanning restaurants, hotels, retail, sports, automotive, and real estate industries.

We have partnered with or are currently associated with several renowned brands, including KFC, Taco Bell, Marriott, Hilton, IHG, Sonic, Little Caesars, Rent-A-Center, Ace Hardware, and Valvoline, among others.

While continuing to expand in the restaurant and hotel industries, we are also keen on diversifying and leveraging our strengths. These include our proven ability to operate successfully within a franchise system, our desire to scale and consolidate by rolling up smaller franchisees, acquiring corporate units through divestiture, and embarking on new unit development. 

Our interest lies in franchise systems across various sectors, including fitness, health & wellness, niche retail, specialty food, sports, automotive, business services, and home services, based on our analysis.

From an investment standpoint, CMG Companies is unique. We have not only owned and operated our businesses but have also invested our own capital and raised funds from limited partners (LPs), including both institutional investors and high-net-worth accredited individuals.

CMG Companies should primarily be recognized as an operator, then as a sponsor investing its own capital, and finally as a family office with a group of partners that invest together. We operate with a partnership model, focusing on long-term investments characterized by a patient approach.
CMG Companies is not a legal entity. CMG Companies is a group of separate and independent legal entities.

Title: Director
Company Product and Services: strategy & governance, strategic leadership for emerging companies, mergers & acquisitions, value-creating growth strategy, venture acceleration, founder coaching, startups
Company Description:
Sage is your Growth Partner

We work with founders, executives, senior teams, and boards of directors to tackle critical growth challenges and drive value creation – at all stages of a company’s lifecycle. 

Sage partners are executives, board members, entrepreneurs, advisors, and business operators. We have been there and seen - and overcome – the same challenges our clients face. 

Sage Partners pursues promising firms for whom we can add tangible enterprise value. We serve young companies seeking to grow beyond the startup phase and established companies needing to accelerate their rate of growth.

Our business model for working with young companies, our Venture Acceleration practice,  is structured around aligning our interests with those of our clients which means being mindful of expenses. Our up-front fees are small. Instead we base our work on a carried interest, often in some form of deferred/performance compensation and/or equity position. Thus we win when and as our clients win. Our interests are aligned.

Our focus for established companies is often at the intersection of strategy and governance, 'when strategy meets governance.'​ Our role is to assist our clients as they seek to ratchet up their rates of growth or to sustain their momentum. Our goal: to achieve value-creating growth, the only kind that counts.
 
We work shoulder-to-shoulder with entrepreneur/founders, executives, and Directors to create valuable growth throughout the life cycle of the organization. Sage is efficient and effective: we bring the right expertise at the right time, tapping into deep know-how and networks. Sage brings a broad and deep network of other professionals with special subject matter and functional expertise. This network helps us to bridge the gap between ideas and action.

Growth and experience matter. Together they create value. How can we help you?

Title: Founder and Principal Technical Project Manager
Company Description:
Welcome to Rocky Mark Ventures, the personal holding company and professional umbrella for my work spanning technical consulting, private investing, and digital content creation.

My career is built on a foundation of 15+ years in Silicon Valley hardware product development and systems architecture. With degrees in Electrical Engineering and Physics, I’ve spent over a decade leading complex, cross-functional engineering initiatives as a Technical Project Manager.

In April 2023, I made the intentional decision to take a planned career break from the traditional corporate track to embrace my most important role: serving as the primary caregiver to my toddler.

Far from a pause in my professional growth, this transition sparked the launch of Rocky Mark Ventures. Under this umbrella, I manage my multifaceted career:

Technical Consulting: I am actively seeking select full-time and consulting opportunities, leveraging my extensive TPM background to help teams execute hardware product development with precision and efficiency.

Private Investing: As an active investor managing my own personal accounts, I specialize in Equity Index Futures. I utilize a "Financial Juxtaposition" strategy—balancing long-term passive index fund investing with active, disciplined daily speculative trading for cash flow.

Content Creation: I document this unique transition from Silicon Valley engineer to stay-at-home dad and active investor. Through YouTube, I share insights on financial transparency, trading education, and the daily realities of managing both family and personal finance.

Rocky Mark Ventures represents the intersection of technical discipline, calculated risk management, and the pursuit of a balanced, self-directed life.

Whether you are looking for an experienced Technical Project Manager to drive your next hardware product to the finish line, or you are simply following along with the financial journey, I’m glad you are here.

Most sourcing agencies play a numbers game. We play a quality game. Our team of analysts acts as your extended front office—conducting cold outreach, verifying seller motivation, and recasting financials (SDE/EBITDA) before a deal ever reaches your desk.

Title: Senior Partner
Company Description:

Title: Senior Partner, Global Market Access and Strategy
Company Description:
SLOANE / Adler is a private advisory firm serving families, sovereign investors, and enterprise leaders who require discretion, judgment, and reach across markets. The firm operates quietly at the intersection of capital, reputation, and state policy, advising on matters where financial interests, public visibility, and institutional relationships converge.

Assignments often involve cross border investment, leadership transition, market entry, reputational exposure, or strategic positioning in sensitive environments. Clients include family offices, senior executives, investors, and public figures seeking clear judgment in moments where decisions carry financial, political, or reputational consequence.

The firm’s advisory approach rests on five areas of counsel.

Capital. Strategic guidance for private transactions, partnerships, and alignment between capital and long term enterprise interests.

Reputation. Counsel on visibility, exposure, and narrative positioning for individuals and institutions operating under public scrutiny.

Stewardship. Advice for family offices and institutions focused on continuity, governance, and responsible leadership across generations.

State and Policy. Strategic perspective on policy environments, sovereign posture, and regulatory context shaping markets and investment flows.

Enterprise and Leadership. Counsel for senior leaders navigating transition, crisis, expansion, or structural change inside complex organizations.

SLOANE / Adler maintains a select circle of senior advisors across government, finance, industry, and international policy. These relationships allow the firm to convene trusted expertise when assignments require deeper reach across sectors or jurisdictions.

The firm operates from Washington and engages across North America, Europe, the Middle East, Asia, and Africa. Discretion, judgment, and long term relationships define the practice.

Title: Operating Executive | Founder
Company Description:
The Industrial Operating System Gap
Many mid-market industrial companies are running on systems designed for a different economic environment.

The symptoms are familiar:
- slow and unpredictable sales cycles
- fragmented commercial data and CRM usage
- manual operational workflows
- legacy capital-equipment revenue models
- limited visibility into pipeline and operational performance

These issues usually seem manageable at first. But over time they create structural friction that slows growth, limits margin expansion, and constrains enterprise value.

The challenge isn’t usually the product.  The challenge is the operating system behind the business.

Operating Thesis
QuandtMLeap is not a traditional consulting firm.

It represents my personal work as an operating executive and transformation partner.

Engagements typically involve working with:
- CEOs of mid-market industrial companies
- private equity operating partners
- leadership teams navigating technology transitions

The work usually begins with diagnosing the structural friction inside a business and then designing the systems required to remove it.

This often includes:
- commercial and operational diagnostics
- revenue operating system design
- RevOps and automation implementation
- leadership alignment around scalable growth
