Title: Founder & Principal
Company Description:
Ficus Advisors is a senior advisory firm for leadership teams navigating the inflection points where value is created or lost.

Companies call us at one of three moments: when first-time institutional capital creates a translation gap between investor language and operating reality; when post-merger integration requires two organizations to combine its people, processes, and culture; or when growth has outpaced the infrastructure underneath it. These situations look different on the surface but the underlying problem is the same - what built the business can't scale it.

Our work at Ficus Advisors focuses on organizational readiness: assessing where a company is, identifying the gap between current state and where it needs to be, and building a clear path to close it. That means leadership development, operating system design, org structure, and go-to-market strategy. But we don’t just deliver a plan, we stay through execution and ensure the leadership team has the support they need to succeed.

Morgan Witham leads every engagement. She spent twelve years advising on M&A transactions at Harris Williams & Co. before joining COLAB as CEO, where she tripled average deal size, rebuilt the go-to-market strategy, scaled the team, and advanced the company into a more durable recurring revenue model. What she advises on, she has done.

By design, Ficus works with a small number of clients at a time. Every client gets senior attention throughout.

Learn more at ficusadvisors.com

Company Description:
ClearGate Advisory provides independent power readiness risk assessments for lenders and private equity investors financing energy-adjacent assets across the Pacific Northwest and Intermountain West.

As interconnection queues extend beyond eight years in some markets and grid constraints tighten across the Western Interconnection, power infrastructure risk has become a material factor in asset valuation and loan underwriting. ClearGate exists to fill that diligence gap.  Delivering structured, credible assessments before capital is committed.

Our work covers six core risk dimensions: interconnection status and queue position, grid dependency and single-point exposure, on-site generation adequacy, regulatory and tariff compliance, consequence mapping, and remediation cost estimation. Each engagement produces a RAG-scored risk report structured for lender and investor review.

ClearGate is led by Crystal Berry, a transactional attorney with deep experience in commercial agreements and infrastructure transactions. ClearGate Advisory is headquartered in Boise, Idaho, and serves clients across the Pacific Northwest and Intermountain West. ClearGate’s assessments are advisory in nature and do not constitute legal advice.

One of EQUATH’s main differentiating factors is its vertical integration capability. Through EQART, its own development company, the firm controls project development, technical execution, construction monitoring and economic control within a single operating structure.

Its model is based on a clear principle: value is not only found in the asset itself, but in the operation built around it.

The key lies in identifying opportunities, structuring risk, controlling execution and transforming new-build hospitality projects into solid, profitable and strategically coherent real estate operations.

EQUATH Private Equity

Title: Director
Company Product and Services: audit defense, state tax audit representation, state tax research
Company Description:
Headquartered in Houston, Texas, Texas Tax Group is a talented team of former Texas Comptroller State Tax Auditors. Texas Tax Group offers a comprehensive range of state tax advisory and consulting services on a multi-jurisdictional basis including but not limited to audit defense, tax appeals, tax compliance, tax recovery, tax credits and incentives and strategic planning.

Title: Senior Managing Director
Company Description:
At HB Capital, we specialize in debt & equity capital placements for commercial real estate investors and developers across the country. As a national capital advisory firm, we connect our clients with the right capital for their unique projects—whether it’s ground-up development, value-add repositioning, or unlocking equity from stabilized assets.

We offer a wide range of loan products tailored to commercial real estate, including:
	•	New Construction Loans – Financing for ground-up development projects across retail, industrial, mixed-use, multifamily, and more.
	•	Bridge Loans – Short-term financing to acquire, reposition, or lease-up commercial properties before permanent financing.
	•	Cash-Out Refinances – Unlock equity from stabilized assets to reinvest in new opportunities or recapitalize.
	•	Land Loans – Acquisition and development financing for entitled and shovel-ready land.
	•	SBA & CMBS Loans – Long-term, competitive-rate financing for owner-users or investment-grade stabilized properties.
	•	Private Capital Solutions – Flexible, asset-based funding from our curated network of private lenders and institutions.

With deep expertise in commercial real estate and strong relationships in the capital markets, HB Capital is known for speed, creativity, and certainty of execution. We work across all major asset classes—including industrial, retail, single-tenant net lease, mixed-use, and land—with loan sizes typically ranging from $2M to $200M+.

Whether you’re scaling your portfolio, building ground-up, or need a creative financing solution in a challenging market, HB Capital is your trusted partner for commercial real estate capital.

Title: Principal
Company Product and Services: mortgage banking, defeasance, investment advisory
Company Description:
Medalist Capital is a commercial real estate financial services company that specializes in mortgage banking, investment advisory and defeasance. Founded in 2004, our commitment and focus on maintaining strong lender relationships has enabled us to become one of the leading real estate financial services firms in the Southeast.

Headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina, with an office in Raleigh, our additional locations include Atlanta, Georgia, Charleston, South Carolina, and Nashville, Tennessee. Although geographically placed in the Southeast, our network of investors and capital sources allow us to service our clients and their properties throughout the United States.

To date, we have successfully completed transactions for over 300 clients and closed more than 2,000 loans, with a deal volume of over $20 billion spread across more than 210 different lenders. Our servicing portfolio is currently in excess of $4 billion. We have closed loans in 39 different states, indicative of the nationwide scope we offer through our network of lenders.

Title: Founder & Strategy Lead
Company Description:
Netra CRB is a regenerative strategy studio for cultural and business transformation. We help brands see deeper and shift further, turning cultural invisibility into influence.

Starting with one of the world’s most undervalued audiences, women 40+, we reframe how aging, identity, and value are understood in culture, leadership, and innovation. We believe regeneration, not inclusion, is the new benchmark for relevance.

Through our proprietary IRIS™ Framework: Insight, Reframe, Intervene, Sustain,  we uncover invisible truths, reshape narratives, and design systems that build long-term equity and impact.

Our Core Expertise:
• Regenerative Brand Strategy – Positioning and narrative design for the new maturity economy.
• Cultural Campaigns & Story Design – Creative strategy that makes maturity visible in culture.
• Insight & Intelligence Tools – AI-supported cultural and behavioral research to reveal emerging narratives.
• Workshops & Leadership Labs – Immersive sessions that transform how teams see, lead, and grow.
• Thought-Leadership Content Studio – Turning purpose into cultural influence.
• Fractional Strategy Partnerships – Embedding regenerative thinking within creative organizations.

We partner with purpose-led brands, creative studios, and investors seeking to align growth with meaning. Our work with Snova Biotech, for example, helped founder Dora Gutierrez Snova build a regenerative brand for the future, one rooted in purpose, culture, and longevity.

Our Mission:
To regenerate how business sees aging, influence, and value creating cultures where visibility becomes vitality, and brands grow not by extraction, but by evolution.

See deeper. Shift further. Regenerate forward.

Company Description:
Liberty Advisor Group is now a Resultant company, uniting senior-led strategic advisory with a 300+-person technical engine to turn complex business challenges into measurable outcomes. 

Liberty Advisor Group is a goal-oriented, client-focused, and results-driven consulting firm. We are a lean, handpicked team of strategists, technologists, and entrepreneurs—battle-tested experts with a steadfast, start-up attitude. By joining forces with Resultant, we have officially eliminated the "execution gap" that often stalls major corporate initiatives. We now provide a single point of accountability that pairs high-level strategic counsel with deep technical expertise in data, AI, technology, and managed services. 

Our team, with an average of 15+ years of experience, has delivered over $1 billion in operating income improvement for our clients. Whether we are navigating a global M&A transaction, leading a high-stakes ERP transition, or optimizing a complex supply chain, we collaborate in real-time to deliver situation-specific solutions that actually work. 

We move beyond high-level "advice" to physically build the centralized data platforms and intelligent systems required to scale an organization. By combining Liberty’s senior practitioners with Resultant’s 300+ technologists, we ensure that technical debt is remediated and infrastructure is stabilized for long-term growth. Liberty Advisor Group has the experience and the technical muscle to realize our clients’ highest ambitions. 

Experienced Teams. Technical Muscle. Verifiable Impact.

Company Description:
Royal Heights Advisory — Federal Go-to-Market for AI, Data, and Cybersecurity

For over ten years I've quietly advised founders, CROs, and federal sales leaders on how to win in the U.S. federal market — alongside the companies I've built federal businesses for. Royal Heights Advisory formalizes that work.
I help emerging and scaling AI, data, and cybersecurity companies do one thing: build federal businesses that last. That means translating technical innovation into mission-aligned capability, navigating the acquisition pathways that decide who gets bought and who gets stuck, and converting early pilots into programs of record.
My career has been spent establishing first-of-kind federal markets — at the earliest stages, before the playbook exists. Across Federal Civilian, National Security, and DoD/DoW environments, I've built the relationships, the deal motions, and the integrator partnerships that turn promising platforms into federal businesses. That operating experience is what I bring to the table as an advisor.
Royal Heights works with CEOs, CROs, and federal leaders who need a partner — not a consultant. Engagements take the shape the business needs: strategic advisor, fractional federal GTM leader, or interim executive through a critical window.
If you're building something the federal government should be buying, let's connect here on LinkedIn.

The firm was established to serve as a parent company, providing strategic oversight and governance across its operating subsidiaries and real estate holdings.

Worthington & Fells acquires and manages high-performing service companies with strong fundamentals and durable cash flow, alongside rental real estate portfolios that generate stable, recurring income. The firm brings an institutional approach to capital allocation, financial management, and operational strategy while allowing each operating business to maintain its brand identity, leadership, and day-to-day autonomy.

Unlike traditional private equity firms, Worthington & Fells is structured for long-term ownership rather than short-term exits. This permanent-capital mindset enables decisive execution, seamless transitions, and responsible stewardship focused on continuity for employees, customers, tenants, and legacy owners. Centralized support functions, including finance, capital strategy, business development, governance, and systems, are provided where they enhance scale and performance without disrupting proven operations.

With a disciplined, solutions-oriented approach and deep operational experience, Worthington & Fells is a trusted partner to brokers, lenders, business owners, and property owners seeking certainty of close, professional execution, and long-term alignment. The firm is committed to building enduring operating companies and real estate platforms that create sustained value for all stakeholders.

Title: Founder & Managing Director
Company Description:
Fractional regulatory, quality, and AI governance for rehab and digital health companies. I translate emerging regulation such as CMS Innovation Center models, FDA SaMD pathways, and AI governance frameworks into commercial and operational strategy your team can execute.

Compliance debt can slow the roadmap, scare enterprise buyers, or surface in fundraising due diligence. Engagements happen at both ends of that curve — preventing debt from forming during scale, or unwinding it before it constrains a deal. Either way, they end with the infrastructure in place and the team running it.

Here's how we can work together:
     1. Embedded partnership where I roll up my sleeves and build compliance and governance frameworks without slowing you down. Most engagements begin with a 4-week diagnostic including a gap analysis, risk-prioritized roadmap, and defined scope for what comes next, before extending into longer-term embedded work. Typical fit: digital health companies scaling operations, FDA engagement, funding due diligence, or building compliance into the roadmap before it becomes a constraint.
     2. Scoped work on specific deliverables. Often a precursor to a fractional partnership. Three categories: compliance infrastructure (HIPAA, BAA frameworks, AI governance), regulatory positioning (FDA SaMD, CMS Innovation Center models, vendor due diligence), and strategic intelligence (market entry, payment model analysis, competitive positioning).
     3. A small number of advisory relationships with early-stage founders building AI and digital health tools for rehabilitation. Typical engagements include: clinical credibility for fundraising and design partner conversations, regulatory exposure assessment before architectural decisions lock in, and ensuring the product reflects how PT actually gets delivered. Typically 2–4 hours/month, available case-by-case where there's a strong fit.

Title: Founder and Chairman of Investment Committee
Company Description:
HC Private Investments is an investment firm founded with the goal of providing individuals and family offices an alternative to the traditional private equity model. With a more patient and flexible capital base, the Firm looks to partner with business owners, executives and management teams to identify opportunities whereby we can remove obstacles to growth enabling companies to unlock their value potential.

With patient investors and a permanent capital base, HC Private Investments has the flexibility to structure and exit investments on a case by case basis. While the Firm focuses on control investments in lower middle market companies, HC Private Investments leverages its ability to offer creative solutions to each opportunity. Investments can also take the form of minority or structured equity. The Firm is not beholden to traditional fund lifecycles and mandatory investment periods. We evaluate each portfolio company and investment opportunity with a focus on future growth initiatives and the ability to reinvest for the long-term.

HC Private Investments believes management is the single most important piece of any investment. Given the Firm’s long-term focus, HC Private Investments seeks to partner with business owners, executives and management teams to develop sustainable growth strategies. The Firm builds consensus and buy-in from its operators prior to executing any investment decision. HC Private Investments supports its management teams with customer introductions, process and procedure implementation, strategy development and operational improvements. In all cases, the Firm relies on management teams to make day-to-day investment decisions to drive growth with HC Private Investment’s team providing strategic board support. Comprised of family offices and former business owners, the Firm’s investors provide insight and guidance to our portfolio companies in a manner uncommon in the private equity industry.

Title: Partner
Company Description:
Forest Avenue Capital Management LP ("FAC" or “the Firm” or “we”) is a global equity long short investment firm with a primary objective to generate superior, consistent returns agnostic of market conditions.  The Firm will aim to achieve this goal by identifying what we believe to be secular themes and highly compelling long and short investment opportunities that are misunderstood, and therefore, mispriced, and possess the most attractive risk-adjusted returns.  FAC’s investment strategy will seek to employ a flexible investment mandate to allow for research results to be expressed across global markets and asset classes, including private investments. The investment universe will cover two primary sector groups:  1) energy, industrials, infrastructure, renewables, and utilities, and 2) consumer.

The Firm will seek to cultivate a cultural foundation built on respect, trust, and alignment of interests, and foster an environment of analysis and debate to reinforce exploratory thought versus confirmatory thought.  FAC is dedicated to world-class, institutional level business operations across both the investment and non-investment functions.  

The Firm was founded by Tarigh Yusufi in 2021.

Company Description:
RJA offers specialized advisory services to pension plans, endowments, outsourced CIOs, and private wealth allocators (including family offices). The general principle behind RJA's approach is to use derivative based solutions to address investor needs, including altering the risk-return profile of the client's existing exposures, hedging against market downturns, creating yield by using concentrated equity positions, efficiently creating cash liquidity, and more.

Company Description:
Perl Hospitality is a Tri-State-based hospitality advisory practice focused on operational infrastructure, scalable systems, and growth strategy for modern restaurant and hospitality brands.

We partner with founders, ownership groups, and executive teams at multi-unit and multi-concept hospitality companies, typically at the stage where growth begins to outpace the systems, reporting, and operational structure supporting the business. Our work centers on building the operational foundation required to scale effectively — strengthening unit economics, streamlining execution, improving visibility across the business, and creating systems that support sustainable expansion without compromising the guest experience or brand identity.

Our engagements generally fall into two core areas. First, we work with emerging and growth-stage hospitality groups to build the operational, financial, and organizational infrastructure needed to scale — including SOP development, labor and profitability optimization, reporting systems, multi-unit operational strategy, franchising groundwork, and capital readiness. Second, we work alongside hospitality technology companies as an operator-first strategic partner, helping align product development, onboarding, implementation, and customer success with the realities of how restaurants and hospitality businesses actually operate.

Recent work has included multi-unit operational restructuring, systems implementation, growth and expansion strategy, franchising preparation, acquisition positioning, and operational optimization across both dine-in and digital channels.

Embarc Advisors is building a disruptive corporate finance advisory firm focused on startups and the middle market. Founded by a former Goldman Sachs investment banker and McKinsey & Co. consultant, Embarc delivers the highest degree of advisory expertise.

Title: Founder & Business Funding Educator
Company Product and Services: business funding, capital strategy, real estate agent consulting, financial positioning, commission income financing, business credit building, capital deployment, business structure coaching, agent capital blueprint, real estate business growth, strategic advisory, investment strategy, credit profile development, small business funding, income stabilization, real estate professionals, wealth building, operator mindset
Company Description:
ALW Strategic Consulting is a strategic funding partner for commission-based real estate agents who are ready to treat their business like a business.

We help growth-minded agents access $25K–$150K+ in usable capital — structured around commission income, built for agents who want to scale their marketing, hire support, and close more deals without depending on last month's commission check.

We are not a traditional lender. We are not a credit repair service. We are an advisory firm that builds the financial architecture that lets agents actually use the capital they qualify for.

Our framework — The Agent Capital Blueprint — moves every client through four phases:
▸ Position: Build the credit profile, entity structure, and documentation that gets you qualified on the right terms.
▸ Fund: Secure the right capital stack, sized to your goals and structured around commission income.
▸ Deploy: Put every dollar to work in the highest-leverage areas: lead generation, income stabilization, and business systems.
▸ Transition: Over 2–5 years, convert commission income and operating leverage into investment capital.

Who we work with: Active agents (1+ year) who are serious about growth and willing to operate like a business owner — not someone looking for a shortcut or a quick approval.

Start with a free Capital Readiness Assessment — a 5-minute evaluation of where you stand and what to do next.

📧 anthowil@alwstrategic.com

Title: President & Co-Founder
Company Description:
Atlas Collective is an operator-led advisory firm focused on one thing: helping companies execute at a higher level.

Most businesses don’t struggle because of a single issue. Growth stalls when vision, revenue, operations, technology, and leadership fall out of alignment. The result is missed targets, inconsistent execution, and complexity that slows the business down.

We fix that.

Our team is made up of experienced operators who have built, run, and scaled companies—not career consultants. We work alongside leadership teams to identify what’s holding the business back, align the core drivers of growth, and build the structure required to scale.

Our approach is grounded in a simple model: align what matters, then execute with discipline.

We engage in three ways:

• Compass Audits to diagnose issues, quantify opportunity, and define a clear path forward
• Transformation Engagements to implement high-impact changes across strategy, revenue, operations, and technology
• Fractional Leadership to provide experienced executive guidance without the overhead of full-time hires

The outcome is measurable: improved execution, more predictable revenue, stronger margins, and leadership teams that operate with clarity and accountability.

We're not here just to deliver presentations. We're here to help businesses run better, grow faster, and scale with confidence.

Atlas Collective works with founder-led companies, growth-stage businesses, and private equity-backed organizations that have outgrown how they operate—and are ready to build what comes next.

Title: Director
Company Product and Services: financing, investing, lower middle market, bdc , private equity, acquisitions
Company Description:
Gladstone Investment Corporation (Nasdaq: GAIN) is the private equity fund managed by The Gladstone Companies, a family of investment funds headquartered in McLean, VA. Gladstone Investment Corporation (“GAIN”) invests in lower middle-market business services, consumer, and industrial companies. We facilitate buyouts and recapitalizations that provide liquidity to sellers and, if desired, we allow for continued ownership through rollover investment and incentive equity pools.
Below are the company characteristics and transaction attributes of our typical investments: 
 
1.	EBITDA between $4-15M (no minimum for add-on opportunities)
2.	Market leading position with sustainable competitive advantages 
3.	Headquartered in the United States 
4.	Recapitalizations of family-owned businesses, management-led buyouts, and corporate divestitures 
5.	Total investment size between $25-75M
 
Total investments are comprised of both debt and equity, which ensures both speed and certainty of closing (no financing contingencies) and fully aligned interests post-close as we are the primary investor. For transactions of larger sizes, we have a strong network of co-investors that we have successfully partnered with on numerous occasions. 

The Gladstone Companies also manages three additional funds. Each fund is a separate entity and led by dedicated teams of investment professionals: 
Gladstone Capital Corporation (Nasdaq: GLAD) is a direct lending fund. 
Gladstone Commercial Corporation (Nasdaq: GOOD) is a commercial property REIT. 
Gladstone Land Corporation (Nasdaq: LAND) is a farmland REIT.

Title: Senior Partner
Company Description:
Eaglewood & Partners is a privately-held investment office. The 
office holds and operates positions across applied artificial 
intelligence, private real estate, marine and aviation assets, 
and selective cross-border advisory mandates — primarily across 
the Brazil–United States–Italy corridor.

The office is small by design. We are not asset managers. We do 
not raise external capital. We do not publish fees, list services, 
or solicit clients. Engagements arise through introduction.

Our applied-AI portfolio is operated through ZeepCode Group 
Technology LLC, our Florida-based operating arm. The portfolio 
includes operating positions in government procurement intelligence, 
clinical aesthetics technology, financial automation for Latin 
American markets, and voice infrastructure.

Our real estate exposure spans South Florida, Lisbon, Cascais, 
and Northern Italy. Our marine and aviation desk operates through 
licensed brokerage partners in Monaco, South Florida, and Lugano.

The office maintains operating presence in Miami, Milan, and 
São Paulo. Eaglewood Foundation, the philanthropic arm of the 
office, supports applied artificial intelligence initiatives in 
education across Lusophone Africa and underserved Brazilian regions.

For inquiries: principal@eaglewoodpartners.com

—

Eaglewood & Partners is a private investment office. The office 
does not provide investment advisory services to the public, does 
not manage external capital, and is not registered as an investment

Title: Co-Founder
Company Description:
Axis Waste Management is a national Waste Service Provider (WSP) managing waste costs, contracts, and billing for multi-location businesses and private equity portfolio companies across all 50 states, Puerto Rico, Alaska, and Hawaii.
We are not a hauler. We are not a broker. A WSP manages your entire waste program — vendors, contracts, invoices, compliance, and cost — as a single accountable partner with no financial relationship with carriers.
Most multi-location businesses are overpaying for waste service. Not because anyone made a bad decision. Because carriers bill with complexity, contracts auto-renew with escalators, and nobody on your team has time to audit 40 invoices a month.
We fix that. Our clients typically recover 10–12% in waste cost savings, with most seeing measurable results within the first 90 days.
Services include vendor and invoice consolidation, contract and invoice auditing, procurement and RFP management, rate increase management, contract lifecycle management, and sustainability and ESG reporting.

Title: Founder & Principal
Company Product and Services: business valuation, strategic advisory, operational analysis, financial analysis, mergers & acquisitions, kpi & margin analysis, growth strategy, exit readiness, performance improvement, financial modeling, owner-operated business
Company Description:
Form + Flow Strategies is a boutique advisory firm focused on operational clarity, valuation insight, and strategic guidance for owner-operated businesses.

The firm was built around a simple belief: the numbers tell the story, but operations determine the outcome. Every engagement combines financial analysis with operational perspective to help business owners better understand what is truly driving performance inside their business.

Form + Flow works with established and growing businesses seeking greater clarity around profitability, operational efficiency, growth readiness, valuation, and long-term decision-making. The firm’s approach blends valuation experience, strategic analysis, and real-world operational understanding into a framework designed to help owners make confident, informed decisions.

Over the past decade, the firm’s founder has completed more than 300 private business valuations across a wide range of industries and supported buy-side, sell-side, succession, and strategic advisory engagements throughout the lower middle market.

The firm operates through three core dimensions:

FORM — Structure. How the business is built.
FLOW — Execution. How the business operates.
FOCUS — Direction. Where the business is going.

Form + Flow Strategies serves owner-operated businesses across Texas and beyond with an emphasis on practical insight, measured execution, and long-term value creation.

Title: Managing Partner
Company Description:
Emberstone Global Risk is an elite enterprise risk management, hazard compliance, and life-safety advisory firm. We architect defensible compliance models and third-party code verification frameworks for institutional portfolios, complex industrial infrastructure, and the renewable energy sector.

Operating at the apex of technical engineering and financial risk securitization, Emberstone translates highly volatile hazard classifications into operational certainty. Our mandate is absolute: to protect institutional capital (including Inflation Reduction Act Section 48 tax equity) by ensuring uncompromising adherence to local, federal, and international safety directives.

Core Advisory Capabilities:

Process Safety Management (PSM): Auditing and compliance mapping for OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 and high-risk volatile processing environments.

Hazardous Area Classification (HazLoc): Third-party verification of explosive environment zoning, including European ATEX Directive 1999/92/EC and DRPCE standards.

Enterprise Risk & OHS: Structural gap analyses and framework validation aligned with ISO 31000 (Risk Management) and ISO 45001 (Occupational Health & Safety).

Capital & Risk Securitization: Aligning life-safety realities with project finance underwriting standards to insulate infrastructure investments from regulatory exposure.

Emberstone operates under strict structural isolation and absolute confidentiality protocols. Leveraging a curated network of premier, independent technical authorities, we deliver unassailable, macro-level compliance verdicts that allow developers and institutional investors to scale operations and deploy capital with absolute confidence.

Defensible Compliance. Operational Certainty.
